1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	require "fastimage"
4	
5	class UploadCreator
6	  TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)
7	
8	  ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
9	    circle
10	    clipPath
11	    defs
12	    ellipse
13	    feGaussianBlur
14	    filter
15	    g
16	    line
17	    linearGradient
18	    marker
19	    path
20	    polygon
21	    polyline
22	    radialGradient
23	    rect
24	    stop
25	    style
26	    svg
27	    text
28	    textPath
29	    tref
30	    tspan
31	    use
32	  ].each(&:freeze)
33	
34	  # Available options
35	  #  - type (string)
36	  #  - origin (string)
37	  #  - for_group_message (boolean)
38	  #  - for_theme (boolean)
39	  #  - for_private_message (boolean)
40	  #  - pasted (boolean)
41	  #  - for_export (boolean)
42	  #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
43	  #  - skip_validations (boolean)
44	  def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
45	    @file = file
46	    @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
47	    @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
48	    @opts = opts
49	    @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
50	    @opts[:validate] = (
51	      if opts[:skip_validations].present?
52	        !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
53	      else
54	        true
55	      end
56	    )
57	  end
58	
59	  def create_for(user_id)
60	    if filesize <= 0
61	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
62	      return @upload
63	    end
64	
65	    @image_info =
66	      begin
67	        image = FastImage.new(@file)
68	        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
69	        image
70	      rescue StandardError
71	        nil
72	      end
73	    is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
74	    is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
75	    is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
76	    is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"
77	
78	    # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
79	    # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
80	    # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
81	    # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
82	    # in S3
83	    #
84	    # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
85	    # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
86	    # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
87	    # place. Needs a refactor.
88	    external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
89	    sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file
90	
91	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
92	      # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
93	      if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
94	        convert_heif!
95	        is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
96	      end
97	
98	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
99	        extract_image_info!
100	        return @upload if @upload.errors.present?
101	
102	        if @image_info.type == :svg
103	          clean_svg!
104	        elsif @image_info.type == :ico
105	          convert_favicon_to_png!
106	        elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
107	          convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
108	          fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
109	          crop! if should_crop?
110	          optimize! if should_optimize?
111	          downsize! if should_downsize?
112	          return @upload if is_still_too_big?
113	        end
114	
115	        # conversion may have switched the type
116	        image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
117	      end
118	
119	      # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
120	      # which is only used for secure uploads
121	      sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
122	      unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
123	        external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail
124	
125	      # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
126	      # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
127	      # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
128	      # access/permission issues for uploads
129	      # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
130	      # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
131	      # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
132	      if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
133	        # do we already have that upload?
134	        @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
135	
136	        # make sure the previous upload has not failed
137	        if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
138	          @upload.destroy
139	          @upload = nil
140	        end
141	
142	        # return the previous upload if any
143	        if @upload
144	          add_metadata!
145	          UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
146	          return @upload
147	        end
148	      end
149	
150	      fixed_original_filename = nil
151	
152	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
153	        current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
154	        expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
155	
156	        # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
157	        # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
158	        # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
159	        if current_extension != expected_extension
160	          basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
161	          fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
162	        end
163	      end
164	
165	      # create the upload otherwise
166	      @upload = Upload.new
167	      @upload.user_id = user_id
168	      @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
169	      @upload.filesize = filesize
170	      @upload.sha1 =
171	        (
172	          if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
173	            unique_hash
174	          else
175	            sha1
176	          end
177	        )
178	      @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
179	      @upload.url = ""
180	      @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
181	      @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]
182	
183	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
184	        if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
185	          w, h = [0, 0]
186	
187	          # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
188	          # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
189	          # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
190	          # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
191	          begin
192	            w, h =
193	              Discourse::Utils
194	                .execute_command(
195	                  "identify",
196	                  "-ping",
197	                  "-format",
198	                  "%w %h",
199	                  "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
200	                  timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
201	                )
202	                .split(" ")
203	                .map(&:to_i)
204	          rescue StandardError
205	            # use default 0, 0
206	          end
207	        else
208	          w, h = @image_info.size
209	        end
210	
211	        @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
212	        @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
213	        @upload.animated = animated?
214	        @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)
215	      end
216	
217	      add_metadata!
218	
219	      if SiteSetting.secure_uploads
220	        secure, reason =
221	          UploadSecurity.new(@upload, @opts.merge(creating: true)).should_be_secure_with_reason
222	        attrs = @upload.secure_params(secure, reason, "upload creator")
223	        @upload.assign_attributes(attrs)
224	      end
225	
226	      # Callbacks using this event to validate the upload or the file must add errors to the
227	      # upload errors object.
228	      #
229	      # Can't do any validation of the file if external_upload_too_big because we don't have
230	      # the actual file.
231	      if !external_upload_too_big
232	        DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, @file, is_image, @upload, @opts[:validate])
233	      end
234	      return @upload unless @upload.errors.empty? && @upload.save(validate: @opts[:validate])
235	
236	      should_move = false
237	      upload_changed =
238	        if external_upload_too_big
239	          false
240	        else
241	          Upload.generate_digest(@file) != sha1_before_changes
242	        end
243	
244	      store = Discourse.store
245	
246	      if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key] && store.external?
247	        should_move = external_upload_too_big || !upload_changed
248	      end
249	
250	      if should_move
251	        # move the file in the store instead of reuploading
252	        url =
253	          store.move_existing_stored_upload(
254	            existing_external_upload_key: @opts[:existing_external_upload_key],
255	            upload: @upload,
256	          )
257	      else
258	        # store the file and update its url
259	        File.open(@file.path) { |f| url = store.store_upload(f, @upload) }
260	        if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
261	          store.delete_file(@opts[:existing_external_upload_key])
262	        end
263	      end
264	
265	      if url.present?
266	        @upload.url = url
267	        @upload.save!(validate: @opts[:validate])
268	      else
269	        @upload.errors.add(
270	          :url,
271	          I18n.t("upload.store_failure", upload_id: @upload.id, user_id: user_id),
272	        )
273	      end
274	
275	      if @upload.errors.empty? && is_image && @opts[:type] == "avatar" && @upload.extension != "svg"
276	        Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: @upload.id)
277	      end
278	
279	      if @upload.errors.empty?
280	        UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
281	      end
282	
283	      @upload
284	    end
285	  ensure
286	    if @file
287	      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
288	    end
289	  end
290	
291	  def extract_image_info!
292	    @image_info =
293	      begin
294	        image = FastImage.new(@file)
295	        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
296	        image
297	      rescue StandardError
298	        nil
299	      end
300	    @file.rewind
301	
302	    if @image_info.nil?
303	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.not_supported_or_corrupted"))
304	    elsif filesize <= 0
305	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
306	    elsif pixels == 0 && @image_info.type.to_s != "svg"
307	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.size_not_found"))
308	    elsif max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
309	      @upload.errors.add(
310	        :base,
311	        I18n.t(
312	          "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
313	          max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
314	          original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
315	        ),
316	      )
317	    end
318	  end
319	
320	  MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG = 1280 * 720
321	
322	  def convert_png_to_jpeg?
323	    return false unless @image_info.type == :png
324	    return true if @opts[:pasted]
325	    return false if SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality == 100
326	    pixels > MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG
327	  end
328	
329	  MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED = 75_000
330	  MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO = 0.70
331	
332	  def convert_favicon_to_png!
333	    png_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .png])
334	
335	    from = @file.path
336	    to = png_tempfile.path
337	
338	    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
339	
340	    from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
341	    to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
342	
343	    from = "#{from}[-1]" # We only want the last(largest) image of the .ico file
344	
345	    opts = { flatten: false } # Preserve transparency
346	
347	    begin
348	      execute_convert(from, to, opts)
349	    rescue StandardError
350	      # retry with debugging enabled
351	      execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
352	    end
353	
354	    @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
355	    @file = png_tempfile
356	    extract_image_info!
357	  end
358	
359	  def convert_to_jpeg!
360	    return if @opts[:for_site_setting]
361	    return if filesize < MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
362	
363	    jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
364	
365	    from = @file.path
366	    to = jpeg_tempfile.path
367	
368	    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
369	
370	    from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
371	    to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
372	
373	    opts = {}
374	
375	    desired_quality = [
376	      SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality,
377	      SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality,
378	    ].compact.min
379	
380	    target_quality = @upload.target_image_quality(from, desired_quality)
381	    opts = { quality: target_quality } if target_quality
382	
383	    begin
384	      execute_convert(from, to, opts)
385	    rescue StandardError
386	      # retry with debugging enabled
387	      execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
388	    end
389	
390	    new_size = File.size(jpeg_tempfile.path)
391	
392	    keep_jpeg = new_size < filesize * MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO
393	    keep_jpeg &&= (filesize - new_size) > MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
394	
395	    if keep_jpeg
396	      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
397	      @file = jpeg_tempfile
398	      extract_image_info!
399	    else
400	      jpeg_tempfile.close!
401	    end
402	  end
403	
404	  def convert_heif_to_jpeg?
405	    File.extname(@filename).downcase.match?(/\.hei(f|c)\z/)
406	  end
407	
408	  def convert_heif!
409	    jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
410	    from = @file.path
411	    to = jpeg_tempfile.path
412	    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
413	
414	    begin
415	      execute_convert(from, to)
416	    rescue StandardError
417	      # retry with debugging enabled
418	      execute_convert(from, to, { debug: true })
419	    end
420	
421	    @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
422	    @file = jpeg_tempfile
423	    extract_image_info!
424	  end
425	
426	  MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS = 20
427	  def execute_convert(from, to, opts = {})
428	    command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]
429	    command << "-flatten" unless opts[:flatten] == false
430	    command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
431	    command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
432	    command << to
433	
434	    Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
435	      *command,
436	      failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
437	      timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
438	    )
439	  end
440	
441	  def should_alter_quality?
442	    return false if animated?
443	
444	    desired_quality =
445	      (
446	        if @image_info.type == :png
447	          SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality
448	        else
449	          SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality
450	        end
451	      )
452	
453	    @upload.target_image_quality(@file.path, desired_quality).present?
454	  end
455	
456	  def should_downsize?
457	    max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size && !animated?
458	  end
459	
460	  def downsize!
461	    3.times do
462	      original_size = filesize
463	      down_tempfile = Tempfile.new(["down", ".#{@image_info.type}"])
464	
465	      from = @file.path
466	      to = down_tempfile.path
467	
468	      OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
469	
470	      OptimizedImage.downsize(from, to, "50%", scale_image: true, raise_on_error: true)
471	
472	      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
473	      @file = down_tempfile
474	
475	      extract_image_info!
476	
477	      return if filesize >= original_size || pixels == 0 || !should_downsize?
478	    end
479	  rescue StandardError
480	    @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.optimize_failure_message"))
481	  end
482	
483	  def is_still_too_big?
484	    if max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
485	      @upload.errors.add(
486	        :base,
487	        I18n.t(
488	          "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
489	          max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
490	          original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
491	        ),
492	      )
493	      true
494	    elsif max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size
495	      @upload.errors.add(
496	        :base,
497	        I18n.t(
498	          "upload.images.too_large_humanized",
499	          max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_image_size),
500	        ),
501	      )
502	      true
503	    else
504	      false
505	    end
506	  end
507	
508	  def clean_svg!
509	    doc = Nokogiri.XML(@file)
510	    doc.xpath(svg_allowlist_xpath).remove
511	    doc.xpath("//@*[starts-with(name(), 'on')]").remove
512	    doc
513	      .css("use")
514	      .each do |use_el|
515	        if use_el.attr("href")
516	          use_el.remove_attribute("href") unless use_el.attr("href").starts_with?("#")
517	        end
518	        use_el.remove_attribute("xlink:href")
519	      end
520	
521	    File.write(@file.path, doc.to_s)
522	    @file.rewind
523	  end
524	
525	  def should_fix_orientation?
526	    # orientation is between 1 and 8, 1 being the default
527	    # cf. http://www.daveperrett.com/articles/2012/07/28/exif-orientation-handling-is-a-ghetto/
528	    @image_info.orientation.to_i > 1
529	  end
530	
531	  MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME = 5
532	  def fix_orientation!
533	    path = @file.path
534	
535	    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(path)
536	    path = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(path, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
537	
538	    Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
539	      "magick",
540	      path,
541	      "-auto-orient",
542	      path,
543	      timeout: MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME,
544	    )
545	
546	    extract_image_info!
547	  end
548	
549	  def should_crop?
550	    if %w[profile_background card_background custom_emoji].include?(@opts[:type]) && animated?
551	      return false
552	    end
553	
554	    TYPES_TO_CROP.include?(@opts[:type])
555	  end
556	
557	  def crop!
558	    max_pixel_ratio = Discourse::PIXEL_RATIOS.max
559	    filename_with_correct_ext = "image.#{@image_info.type}"
560	
561	    case @opts[:type]
562	    when "avatar"
563	      width = height = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
564	      OptimizedImage.resize(
565	        @file.path,
566	        @file.path,
567	        width,
568	        height,
569	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
570	      )
571	    when "profile_background"
572	      max_width = 850 * max_pixel_ratio
573	      width, height =
574	        ImageSizer.resize(
575	          @image_info.size[0],
576	          @image_info.size[1],
577	          max_width: max_width,
578	          max_height: max_width,
579	        )
580	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
581	        @file.path,
582	        @file.path,
583	        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
584	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
585	      )
586	    when "card_background"
587	      max_width = 590 * max_pixel_ratio
588	      width, height =
589	        ImageSizer.resize(
590	          @image_info.size[0],
591	          @image_info.size[1],
592	          max_width: max_width,
593	          max_height: max_width,
594	        )
595	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
596	        @file.path,
597	        @file.path,
598	        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
599	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
600	      )
601	    when "custom_emoji"
602	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
603	        @file.path,
604	        @file.path,
605	        "100x100\>",
606	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
607	      )
608	    end
609	
610	    extract_image_info!
611	  end
612	
613	  def should_optimize?
614	    # GIF is too slow (plus, we'll soon be converting them to MP4)
615	    # Optimizing SVG is useless
616	    return false if @file.path =~ /\.(gif|svg)\z/i
617	    # Safeguard for large PNGs
618	    return pixels < 2_000_000 if @file.path =~ /\.png/i
619	    # Everything else is fine!
620	    true
621	  end
622	
623	  def optimize!
624	    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)
625	    FileHelper.optimize_image!(@file.path)
626	    extract_image_info!
627	  end
628	
629	  def filesize
630	    @filesize || File.size?(@file.path).to_i
631	  end
632	
633	  def max_image_size
634	    @max_image_size ||= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
635	  end
636	
637	  def max_image_pixels
638	    @max_image_pixels ||= SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels * 1_000_000
639	  end
640	
641	  def pixels
642	    @image_info.size&.reduce(:*).to_i
643	  end
644	
645	  def svg_allowlist_xpath
646	    @@svg_allowlist_xpath ||=
647	      "//*[#{ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS.map { |e| "name()!='#{e}'" }.join(" and ")}]"
648	  end
649	
650	  def add_metadata!
651	    @upload.for_private_message = true if @opts[:for_private_message]
652	    @upload.for_group_message = true if @opts[:for_group_message]
653	    @upload.for_theme = true if @opts[:for_theme]
654	    @upload.for_export = true if @opts[:for_export]
655	    @upload.for_site_setting = true if @opts[:for_site_setting]
656	    @upload.site_setting_name = @opts[:site_setting_name] if @opts[:site_setting_name]
657	    @upload.for_gravatar = true if @opts[:for_gravatar]
658	  end
659	
660	  private
661	
662	  def animated?
663	    return @animated if @animated != nil
664	
665	    @animated ||=
666	      begin
667	        is_animated = FastImage.animated?(@file)
668	        type = @image_info.type.to_s
669	
670	        if is_animated != nil
671	          # FastImage will return nil if it cannot determine if animated
672	          is_animated
673	        elsif %w[gif webp avif].include?(type)
674	          # Only GIFs, WEBPs and a few other unsupported image types can be animated
675	          OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)
676	
677	          command = ["identify", "-ping", "-format", "%n\\n", @file.path]
678	          frames =
679	            begin
680	              Discourse::Utils.execute_command(*command, timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS).to_i
681	            rescue StandardError
682	              1
683	            end
684	
685	          frames > 1
686	        else
687	          false
688	        end
689	      end
690	  end
691	
692	  def generate_fake_sha1_hash
693	    SecureRandom.hex(20)
694	  end
695	end

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "fastimage"

class UploadCreator
  TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)

  ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
    circle
    clipPath
    defs
    ellipse
    feGaussianBlur
    filter
    g
    line
    linearGradient
    marker
    path
    polygon
    polyline
    radialGradient
    rect
    stop
    style
    svg
    text
    textPath
    tref
    tspan
    use
  ].each(&:freeze)

  # Available options
  #  - type (string)
  #  - origin (string)
  #  - for_group_message (boolean)
  #  - for_theme (boolean)
  #  - for_private_message (boolean)
  #  - pasted (boolean)
  #  - for_export (boolean)
  #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
  #  - skip_validations (boolean)
  def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
    @file = file
    @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
    @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
    @opts = opts
    @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    @opts[:validate] = (
      if opts[:skip_validations].present?
        !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
      else
        true
      end
    )
  end

  def create_for(user_id)
    if filesize <= 0
      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
      return @upload
    end

    @image_info =
      begin
        image = FastImage.new(@file)
        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
        image
      rescue StandardError
        nil
      end
    is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
    is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
    is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
    is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"

    # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
    # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
    # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
    # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
    # in S3
    #
    # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
    # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
    # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
    # place. Needs a refactor.
    external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file

    DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
      # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
      if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
        convert_heif!
        is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
      end

      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
        extract_image_info!
        return @upload if @upload.errors.present?

        if @image_info.type == :svg
          clean_svg!
        elsif @image_info.type == :ico
          convert_favicon_to_png!
        elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
          convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
          fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
          crop! if should_crop?
          optimize! if should_optimize?
          downsize! if should_downsize?
          return @upload if is_still_too_big?
        end

        # conversion may have switched the type
        image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
      end

      # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
      # which is only used for secure uploads
      sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
      unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
        external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail

      # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
      # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
      # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
      # access/permission issues for uploads
      # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
      # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
      # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
      if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
        # do we already have that upload?
        @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)

        # make sure the previous upload has not failed
        if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
          @upload.destroy
          @upload = nil
        end

        # return the previous upload if any
        if @upload
          add_metadata!
          UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
          return @upload
        end
      end

      fixed_original_filename = nil

      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
        current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
        expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")

        # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
        # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
        # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
        if current_extension != expected_extension
          basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
          fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
        end
      end

      # create the upload otherwise
      @upload = Upload.new
      @upload.user_id = user_id
      @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
      @upload.filesize = filesize
      @upload.sha1 =
        (
          if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
            unique_hash
          else
            sha1
          end
        )
      @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
      @upload.url = ""
      @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
      @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]

      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
        if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
          w, h = [0, 0]

          # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
          # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
          # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
          # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
          begin
            w, h =
              Discourse::Utils
                .execute_command(
                  "identify",
                  "-ping",
                  "-format",
                  "%w %h",
                  "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
                  timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
                )
                .split(" ")
                .map(&:to_i)
          rescue StandardError
            # use default 0, 0
          end
        else
          w, h = @image_info.size
        end

        @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
        @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
        @upload.animated = animated?
        @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)
      end

      add_metadata!

      if SiteSetting.secure_uploads
        secure, reason =
          UploadSecurity.new(@upload, @opts.merge(creating: true)).should_be_secure_with_reason
        attrs = @upload.secure_params(secure, reason, "upload creator")
        @upload.assign_attributes(attrs)
      end

      # Callbacks using this event to validate the upload or the file must add errors to the
      # upload errors object.
      #
      # Can't do any validation of the file if external_upload_too_big because we don't have
      # the actual file.
      if !external_upload_too_big
        DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, @file, is_image, @upload, @opts[:validate])
      end
      return @upload unless @upload.errors.empty? && @upload.save(validate: @opts[:validate])

      should_move = false
      upload_changed =
        if external_upload_too_big
          false
        else
          Upload.generate_digest(@file) != sha1_before_changes
        end

      store = Discourse.store

      if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key] && store.external?
        should_move = external_upload_too_big || !upload_changed
      end

      if should_move
        # move the file in the store instead of reuploading
        url =
          store.move_existing_stored_upload(
            existing_external_upload_key: @opts[:existing_external_upload_key],
            upload: @upload,
          )
      else
        # store the file and update its url
        File.open(@file.path) { |f| url = store.store_upload(f, @upload) }
        if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
          store.delete_file(@opts[:existing_external_upload_key])
        end
      end

      if url.present?
        @upload.url = url
        @upload.save!(validate: @opts[:validate])
      else
        @upload.errors.add(
          :url,
          I18n.t("upload.store_failure", upload_id: @upload.id, user_id: user_id),
        )
      end

      if @upload.errors.empty? && is_image && @opts[:type] == "avatar" && @upload.extension != "svg"
        Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: @upload.id)
      end

      if @upload.errors.empty?
        UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
      end

      @upload
    end
  ensure
    if @file
      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
    end
  end

  def extract_image_info!
    @image_info =
      begin
        image = FastImage.new(@file)
        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
        image
      rescue StandardError
        nil
      end
    @file.rewind

    if @image_info.nil?
      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.not_supported_or_corrupted"))
    elsif filesize <= 0
      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
    elsif pixels == 0 && @image_info.type.to_s != "svg"
      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.size_not_found"))
    elsif max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
      @upload.errors.add(
        :base,
        I18n.t(
          "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
          max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
          original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
        ),
      )
    end
  end

  MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG = 1280 * 720

  def convert_png_to_jpeg?
    return false unless @image_info.type == :png
    return true if @opts[:pasted]
    return false if SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality == 100
    pixels > MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG
  end

  MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED = 75_000
  MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO = 0.70

  def convert_favicon_to_png!
    png_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .png])

    from = @file.path
    to = png_tempfile.path

    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

    from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
    to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)

    from = "#{from}[-1]" # We only want the last(largest) image of the .ico file

    opts = { flatten: false } # Preserve transparency

    begin
      execute_convert(from, to, opts)
    rescue StandardError
      # retry with debugging enabled
      execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
    end

    @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
    @file = png_tempfile
    extract_image_info!
  end

  def convert_to_jpeg!
    return if @opts[:for_site_setting]
    return if filesize < MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED

    jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])

    from = @file.path
    to = jpeg_tempfile.path

    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

    from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
    to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)

    opts = {}

    desired_quality = [
      SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality,
      SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality,
    ].compact.min

    target_quality = @upload.target_image_quality(from, desired_quality)
    opts = { quality: target_quality } if target_quality

    begin
      execute_convert(from, to, opts)
    rescue StandardError
      # retry with debugging enabled
      execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
    end

    new_size = File.size(jpeg_tempfile.path)

    keep_jpeg = new_size < filesize * MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO
    keep_jpeg &&= (filesize - new_size) > MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED

    if keep_jpeg
      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
      @file = jpeg_tempfile
      extract_image_info!
    else
      jpeg_tempfile.close!
    end
  end

  def convert_heif_to_jpeg?
    File.extname(@filename).downcase.match?(/\.hei(f|c)\z/)
  end

  def convert_heif!
    jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
    from = @file.path
    to = jpeg_tempfile.path
    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

    begin
      execute_convert(from, to)
    rescue StandardError
      # retry with debugging enabled
      execute_convert(from, to, { debug: true })
    end

    @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
    @file = jpeg_tempfile
    extract_image_info!
  end

  MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS = 20
  def execute_convert(from, to, opts = {})
    command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]
    command << "-flatten" unless opts[:flatten] == false
    command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
    command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
    command << to

    Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
      *command,
      failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
      timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
    )
  end

  def should_alter_quality?
    return false if animated?

    desired_quality =
      (
        if @image_info.type == :png
          SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality
        else
          SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality
        end
      )

    @upload.target_image_quality(@file.path, desired_quality).present?
  end

  def should_downsize?
    max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size && !animated?
  end

  def downsize!
    3.times do
      original_size = filesize
      down_tempfile = Tempfile.new(["down", ".#{@image_info.type}"])

      from = @file.path
      to = down_tempfile.path

      OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

      OptimizedImage.downsize(from, to, "50%", scale_image: true, raise_on_error: true)

      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
      @file = down_tempfile

      extract_image_info!

      return if filesize >= original_size || pixels == 0 || !should_downsize?
    end
  rescue StandardError
    @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.optimize_failure_message"))
  end

  def is_still_too_big?
    if max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
      @upload.errors.add(
        :base,
        I18n.t(
          "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
          max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
          original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
        ),
      )
      true
    elsif max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size
      @upload.errors.add(
        :base,
        I18n.t(
          "upload.images.too_large_humanized",
          max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_image_size),
        ),
      )
      true
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def clean_svg!
    doc = Nokogiri.XML(@file)
    doc.xpath(svg_allowlist_xpath).remove
    doc.xpath("//@*[starts-with(name(), 'on')]").remove
    doc
      .css("use")
      .each do |use_el|
        if use_el.attr("href")
          use_el.remove_attribute("href") unless use_el.attr("href").starts_with?("#")
        end
        use_el.remove_attribute("xlink:href")
      end

    File.write(@file.path, doc.to_s)
    @file.rewind
  end

  def should_fix_orientation?
    # orientation is between 1 and 8, 1 being the default
    # cf. http://www.daveperrett.com/articles/2012/07/28/exif-orientation-handling-is-a-ghetto/
    @image_info.orientation.to_i > 1
  end

  MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME = 5
  def fix_orientation!
    path = @file.path

    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(path)
    path = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(path, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")

    Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
      "magick",
      path,
      "-auto-orient",
      path,
      timeout: MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME,
    )

    extract_image_info!
  end

  def should_crop?
    if %w[profile_background card_background custom_emoji].include?(@opts[:type]) && animated?
      return false
    end

    TYPES_TO_CROP.include?(@opts[:type])
  end

  def crop!
    max_pixel_ratio = Discourse::PIXEL_RATIOS.max
    filename_with_correct_ext = "image.#{@image_info.type}"

    case @opts[:type]
    when "avatar"
      width = height = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
      OptimizedImage.resize(
        @file.path,
        @file.path,
        width,
        height,
        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
      )
    when "profile_background"
      max_width = 850 * max_pixel_ratio
      width, height =
        ImageSizer.resize(
          @image_info.size[0],
          @image_info.size[1],
          max_width: max_width,
          max_height: max_width,
        )
      OptimizedImage.downsize(
        @file.path,
        @file.path,
        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
      )
    when "card_background"
      max_width = 590 * max_pixel_ratio
      width, height =
        ImageSizer.resize(
          @image_info.size[0],
          @image_info.size[1],
          max_width: max_width,
          max_height: max_width,
        )
      OptimizedImage.downsize(
        @file.path,
        @file.path,
        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
      )
    when "custom_emoji"
      OptimizedImage.downsize(
        @file.path,
        @file.path,
        "100x100\>",
        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
      )
    end

    extract_image_info!
  end

  def should_optimize?
    # GIF is too slow (plus, we'll soon be converting them to MP4)
    # Optimizing SVG is useless
    return false if @file.path =~ /\.(gif|svg)\z/i
    # Safeguard for large PNGs
    return pixels < 2_000_000 if @file.path =~ /\.png/i
    # Everything else is fine!
    true
  end

  def optimize!
    OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)
    FileHelper.optimize_image!(@file.path)
    extract_image_info!
  end

  def filesize
    @filesize || File.size?(@file.path).to_i
  end

  def max_image_size
    @max_image_size ||= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
  end

  def max_image_pixels
    @max_image_pixels ||= SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels * 1_000_000
  end

  def pixels
    @image_info.size&.reduce(:*).to_i
  end

  def svg_allowlist_xpath
    @@svg_allowlist_xpath ||=
      "//*[#{ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS.map { |e| "name()!='#{e}'" }.join(" and ")}]"
  end

  def add_metadata!
    @upload.for_private_message = true if @opts[:for_private_message]
    @upload.for_group_message = true if @opts[:for_group_message]
    @upload.for_theme = true if @opts[:for_theme]
    @upload.for_export = true if @opts[:for_export]
    @upload.for_site_setting = true if @opts[:for_site_setting]
    @upload.site_setting_name = @opts[:site_setting_name] if @opts[:site_setting_name]
    @upload.for_gravatar = true if @opts[:for_gravatar]
  end

  private

  def animated?
    return @animated if @animated != nil

    @animated ||=
      begin
        is_animated = FastImage.animated?(@file)
        type = @image_info.type.to_s

        if is_animated != nil
          # FastImage will return nil if it cannot determine if animated
          is_animated
        elsif %w[gif webp avif].include?(type)
          # Only GIFs, WEBPs and a few other unsupported image types can be animated
          OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)

          command = ["identify", "-ping", "-format", "%n\\n", @file.path]
          frames =
            begin
              Discourse::Utils.execute_command(*command, timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS).to_i
            rescue StandardError
              1
            end

          frames > 1
        else
          false
        end
      end
  end

  def generate_fake_sha1_hash
    SecureRandom.hex(20)
  end
end

3	##
4	# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
5	#
6	# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
7	# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
8	# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
9	# option is present.
10	#
11	# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
12	# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
13	# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
14	# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
15	# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
16	# complex queries and joins to perform.
17	#
18	# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
19	# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
20	# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
21	# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
22	#
23	# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
24	# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
25	# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
26	# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
27	class UploadSecurity
28	  @@custom_public_types = []
29	
30	  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
31	    avatar
32	    custom_emoji
33	    profile_background
34	    card_background
35	    category_logo
36	    category_logo_dark
37	    category_background
38	    group_flair
39	    badge_image
40	    site_setting
41	  ]
42	
43	  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
44	    Badge
45	    Category
46	    CustomEmoji
47	    Group
48	    SiteSetting
49	    ThemeField
50	    User
51	    UserAvatar
52	    UserProfile
53	  ]
54	
55	  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
56	    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
57	  end
58	
59	  # used in tests
60	  def self.reset_custom_public_types
61	    @@custom_public_types = []
62	  end
63	
64	  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
65	    @upload = upload
66	    @opts = opts
67	    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
68	    @creating = @opts[:creating]
69	  end
70	
71	  def should_be_secure?
72	    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
73	  end
74	
75	  def should_be_secure_with_reason
76	    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
77	    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
78	      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
79	      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
80	    end
81	
82	    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
83	  end
84	
85	  private
86	
87	  def access_control_post
88	    @access_control_post ||=
89	      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
90	  end
91	
92	  def insecure_context_checks
93	    {
94	      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
95	      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
96	      public_type: "upload is public type",
97	      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
98	      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
99	    }
100	  end
101	
102	  def secure_context_checks
103	    {
104	      login_required: "login is required",
105	      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
106	      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
107	      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
108	      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
109	    }
110	  end
111	
112	  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
113	  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
114	  # immediately if there is an access control post.
115	  def priority_check?(check)
116	    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
117	  end
118	
119	  def perform_check(check)
120	    send("#{check}_check")
121	  end
122	
123	  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
124	
125	  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
126	    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
127	  end
128	
129	  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
130	    return false if !@creating
131	    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
132	  end
133	
134	  def public_type_check
135	    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
136	  end
137	
138	  def publicly_referenced_first_check
139	    return false if @creating
140	    first_reference =
141	      @upload
142	        .upload_references
143	        .joins(<<~SQL)
144	          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
145	        SQL
146	        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
147	        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
148	        .first
149	    return false if first_reference.blank?
150	    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
151	  end
152	
153	  def regular_emoji_check
154	    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
155	    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
156	    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
157	    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
158	  end
159	
160	  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
161	
162	  #--------------------------#
163	
164	  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
165	
166	  def login_required_check
167	    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
168	  end
169	
170	  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
171	  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
172	  #
173	  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
174	  #
175	  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
176	  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
177	  #
178	  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
179	  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
180	  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
181	    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
182	  end
183	
184	  def uploading_in_composer_check
185	    @upload_type == "composer"
186	  end
187	
188	  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
189	    return false if !@creating
190	    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
191	  end
192	
193	  def already_secure_check
194	    @upload.secure?
195	  end
196	
197	  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
198	end

# frozen_string_literal: true

##
# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
#
# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
# option is present.
#
# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
# complex queries and joins to perform.
#
# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
#
# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
class UploadSecurity
  @@custom_public_types = []

  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
    avatar
    custom_emoji
    profile_background
    card_background
    category_logo
    category_logo_dark
    category_background
    group_flair
    badge_image
    site_setting
  ]

  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
    Badge
    Category
    CustomEmoji
    Group
    SiteSetting
    ThemeField
    User
    UserAvatar
    UserProfile
  ]

  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
  end

  # used in tests
  def self.reset_custom_public_types
    @@custom_public_types = []
  end

  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
    @upload = upload
    @opts = opts
    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
    @creating = @opts[:creating]
  end

  def should_be_secure?
    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
  end

  def should_be_secure_with_reason
    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
    end

    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
  end

  private

  def access_control_post
    @access_control_post ||=
      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
  end

  def insecure_context_checks
    {
      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
      public_type: "upload is public type",
      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
    }
  end

  def secure_context_checks
    {
      login_required: "login is required",
      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
    }
  end

  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
  # immediately if there is an access control post.
  def priority_check?(check)
    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
  end

  def perform_check(check)
    send("#{check}_check")
  end

  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####

  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
  end

  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
    return false if !@creating
    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
  end

  def public_type_check
    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
  end

  def publicly_referenced_first_check
    return false if @creating
    first_reference =
      @upload
        .upload_references
        .joins(<<~SQL)
          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
        SQL
        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
        .first
    return false if first_reference.blank?
    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
  end

  def regular_emoji_check
    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
  end

  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####

  #--------------------------#

  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####

  def login_required_check
    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
  end

  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
  #
  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
  #
  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
  #
  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
  end

  def uploading_in_composer_check
    @upload_type == "composer"
  end

  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
    return false if !@creating
    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
  end

  def already_secure_check
    @upload.secure?
  end

  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module FileStore
  class DownloadError < StandardError
  end

  class BaseStore
    UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX = %r{/(original/\d+X/.*)}
    OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX = %r{/(optimized/\d+X/.*)}
    TEMPORARY_UPLOAD_PREFIX = "temp/"

    def store_upload(file, upload, content_type = nil)
      upload.url = nil
      path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
      store_file(file, path)
    end

    def store_optimized_image(file, optimized_image, content_type = nil, secure: false)
      optimized_image.url = nil
      path = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
      store_file(file, path)
    end

    def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
      not_implemented
    end

    def remove_upload(upload)
      remove_file(upload.url, get_path_for_upload(upload))
    end

    def remove_optimized_image(optimized_image)
      remove_file(optimized_image.url, get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image))
    end

    def remove_file(url, path)
      not_implemented
    end

    def upload_path
      path = File.join("uploads", RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db)
      return path if !Rails.env.test?
      File.join(path, "test_#{ENV["TEST_ENV_NUMBER"].presence || "0"}")
    end

    def self.temporary_upload_path(file_name, folder_prefix: "")
      # We don't want to use the original file name as it can contain special
      # characters, which can interfere with external providers operations and
      # introduce other unexpected behaviour.
      file_name_random = "#{SecureRandom.hex}#{File.extname(file_name)}"
      File.join(TEMPORARY_UPLOAD_PREFIX, folder_prefix, SecureRandom.hex, file_name_random)
    end

    def has_been_uploaded?(url)
      not_implemented
    end

    def download_url(upload)
      not_implemented
    end

    def cdn_url(url)
      not_implemented
    end

    def absolute_base_url
      not_implemented
    end

    def relative_base_url
      not_implemented
    end

    def s3_upload_host
      not_implemented
    end

    def external?
      not_implemented
    end

    def internal?
      !external?
    end

    def path_for(upload)
      not_implemented
    end

    def list_missing_uploads(skip_optimized: false)
      not_implemented
    end

    def download(*, **)
      perform_download(*, **)
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end

    def download_safe(*, **)
      Discourse.deprecate(
        "`FileStore::BaseStore#download_safe` is deprecated. Use `#download` instead.",
        since: "2026.02",
        drop_from: "2026.08",
      )
      download(*, **)
    end

    def download!(*, **)
      perform_download(*, **)
    rescue StandardError
      raise DownloadError
    end

    def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
    end

    def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
      depth = get_depth_for(id)
      tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")
      "#{type}/#{depth + 1}X/#{tree}#{sha}#{extension}"
    end

    def get_path_for_upload(upload)
      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
      if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
        return prefix_path(path)
      end

      extension =
        if upload.extension
          ".#{upload.extension}"
        else
          # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
          File.extname(upload.original_filename)
        end

      get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
    end

    def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
      if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
        return prefix_path(path)
      end

      upload = optimized_image.upload
      version = optimized_image.version || 1
      extension =
        "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
      get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
    end

    CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
    CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
    CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100

    def get_cache_path_for(filename)
      "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
    end

    def get_from_cache(filename)
      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
      path if File.exist?(path)
    end

    def cache_file(file, filename)
      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
      dir = File.dirname(path)
      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
      FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)

      DiskCacheEviction.evict(
        dir: CACHE_DIR,
        max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
        evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
      )
    end

    private

    def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
      DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
        name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
        extension = File.extname(name)
        filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"

        unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
          max_file_size_kb ||= [
            SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
            SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
          ].max.kilobytes

          if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
            url =
              Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
          else
            url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
          end

          url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}

          file =
            FileHelper.download(
              url,
              max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
              tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
              follow_redirect: true,
            )

          return if file.nil?

          cache_file(file, filename)
        end

        get_from_cache(filename)
      end
    end

    def not_implemented
      raise "Not implemented."
    end

    def get_depth_for(id)
      depths = [0]
      depths << Math.log(id / 1_000.0, 16).ceil if id.positive?
      depths.max
    end

    def prefix_path(path)
      path
    end
  end
end

@post is not available (e.g., for chat messages) 489 return true if @post.nil? 490 491 @hotlinked_map ||= @post.post_hotlinked_media.preload(:upload).index_by(&:url) 492 normalized_src = 493 PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(img["src"] || img[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR]) 494 info = @hotlinked_map[normalized_src] 495 496 still_an_image = true 497 498 if info&.too_large? 499 if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1 500 add_large_image_placeholder!(img) 501 else 502 img.remove 503 end 504 505 still_an_image = false 506 elsif info&.download_failed? 507 if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1 508 add_broken_image_placeholder!(img) 509 else 510 img.remove 511 end 512 513 still_an_image = false 514 elsif info&.downloaded? && upload = info&.upload 515 img["src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) 516 img["data-dominant-color"] = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence 517 img.delete(PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR) 518 end 519 520 still_an_image 521 end 522 523 def optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: false) 524 w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i 525 onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first 526 527 # note: optimize_urls cooks the src further after this 528 thumbnail = upload.thumbnail(w, h) 529 if thumbnail && thumbnail.filesize.to_i < upload.filesize 530 img["src"] = thumbnail.url 531 532 srcset = +"" 533 534 # Skip srcset for onebox images. Because onebox thumbnails by default 535 # are fairly small the width/height of the smallest thumbnail is likely larger 536 # than what the onebox thumbnail size will be displayed at, so we shouldn't 537 # need to upscale for retina devices 538 if !onebox 539 each_responsive_ratio do |ratio| 540 resized_w = (w * ratio).to_i 541 resized_h = (h * ratio).to_i 542 543 if !cropped && upload.width && resized_w > upload.width 544 cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) 545 srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x" 546 elsif t = upload.thumbnail(resized_w, resized_h) 547 cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(t.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) 548 srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x" 549 end 550 551 img[ 552 "srcset" 553 ] = "#{UrlHelper.cook_url(img["src"], secure: @should_secure_uploads)}#{srcset}" if srcset.present? 554 end 555 end 556 else 557 img["src"] = upload.url 558 end 559 560 if !@disable_dominant_color && 561 (color = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence) 562 img["data-dominant-color"] = color 563 end 564 end 565 566 def add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop) 567 return if original_width < MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH || original_height < MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT 568 569 # first, create a div to hold our lightbox 570 lightbox = create_node("div", LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS) 571 img.add_next_sibling(lightbox) 572 lightbox.add_child(img) 573 574 # then, the link to our larger image 575 src_url = Upload.secure_uploads_url?(img["src"]) ? upload&.url || img["src"] : img["src"] 576 src = UrlHelper.cook_url(src_url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) 577 578 a = create_link_node("lightbox", src) 579 img.add_next_sibling(a) 580 581 a["data-download-href"] = Discourse.store.download_url(upload) if upload 582 583 a.add_child(img) 584 585 # then, some overlay informations 586 meta = create_node("div", "meta") 587 img.add_next_sibling(meta) 588 589 filename = get_filename(upload, img["src"]) 590 informations = +"#{original_width}×#{original_height}" 591 informations << " #{upload.human_filesize}" if upload 592 593 a["title"] = img["title"] || img["alt"] || filename 594 595 meta.add_child create_icon_node("far-image") 596 meta.add_child create_span_node("filename", a["title"]) 597 meta.add_child create_span_node("informations", informations) 598 meta.add_child create_icon_node("discourse-expand") 599 end 600 end 601
