# frozen_string_literal: true module CookedProcessorMixin GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/} LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper" MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100 MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100 def post_process_oneboxes limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size oneboxes = {} inlineOneboxes = {} Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element| is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url] if skip_onebox if is_onebox element.remove_class("onebox") else remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element) end next end limit -= 1 map[url] = true if is_onebox onebox = Oneboxer.onebox( url, invalidate_oneboxes: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes], user_id: @model&.user_id, category_id: @category_id, ) @has_oneboxes = true if onebox.present? onebox else process_inline_onebox(element) false end end PrettyText.sanitize_hotlinked_media(@doc) oneboxed_images.each do |img| next if img["src"].blank? parent = img.parent if respond_to?(:process_hotlinked_image, true) still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img) next if !still_an_image end # make sure we grab dimensions for oneboxed images # and wrap in a div limit_size!(img) next if img["class"]&.include?("onebox-avatar") parent = parent&.parent if parent&.name == "a" parent_class = parent && parent["class"] width = img["width"].to_i height = img["height"].to_i if parent_class&.include?("onebox-body") && width > 0 && height > 0 # special instruction for width == height, assume we are dealing with an avatar if (img["width"].to_i == img["height"].to_i) found = false parent = img while parent = parent.parent if parent["class"] && parent["class"].match?(/\b(allowlistedgeneric|discoursetopic)\b/) found = true break end end if found img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-avatar" next end end if width < 64 && height < 64 img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-full-image" else img.delete("width") img.delete("height") new_parent = img.add_next_sibling( "
", ) new_parent.first.add_child(img) end elsif ( parent_class&.include?("instagram-images") || parent_class&.include?("tweet-images") || parent_class&.include?("scale-images") ) && width > 0 && height > 0 img.remove_attribute("width") img.remove_attribute("height") parent["class"] = "aspect-image-full-size" parent["style"] = "--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};" end end if @omit_nofollow || !SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content @doc .css(".onebox-body a[rel], .onebox a[rel]") .each do |a| rel_values = a["rel"].split(" ").map(&:downcase) rel_values.delete("nofollow") rel_values.delete("ugc") if rel_values.blank? a.remove_attribute("rel") else a["rel"] = rel_values.join(" ") end end end end def post_process_images extract_images.each do |img| still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img) convert_to_link!(img) if still_an_image end end def extract_images # all images with a src attribute @doc.css("img[src], img[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]") - # minus data images @doc.css("img[src^='data']") - # minus emojis @doc.css("img.emoji") end def limit_size!(img) # retrieve the size from # 1) the width/height attributes # 2) the dimension from the preview (image_sizes) # 3) the dimension of the original image (HTTP request) w, h = get_size_from_attributes(img) || get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes]) || get_size(img["src"]) # limit the size of the thumbnail img["width"], img["height"] = ImageSizer.resize(w, h) end def get_size_from_attributes(img) w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i return w, h if w > 0 && h > 0 # if only width or height are specified attempt to scale image if w > 0 || h > 0 w = w.to_f h = h.to_f return unless original_image_size = get_size(img["src"]) original_width, original_height = original_image_size.map(&:to_f) if w > 0 ratio = w / original_width [w.floor, (original_height * ratio).floor] else ratio = h / original_height [(original_width * ratio).floor, h.floor] end end end def get_size_from_image_sizes(src, image_sizes) return if image_sizes.blank? image_sizes.each do |image_size| url, size = image_size[0], image_size[1] if url && src && url.include?(src) && size && size["width"].to_i > 0 && size["height"].to_i > 0 return size["width"], size["height"] end end nil end def add_to_size_cache(url, w, h) @size_cache[url] = [w, h] end def get_size(url) return @size_cache[url] if @size_cache.has_key?(url) absolute_url = url absolute_url = Discourse.base_url_no_prefix + absolute_url if absolute_url =~ %r{\A/[^/]} return unless absolute_url # FastImage fails when there's no scheme absolute_url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + absolute_url if absolute_url.start_with?("//") # we can't direct FastImage to our secure-uploads url because it bounces # anonymous requests with a 404 error if url && Upload.secure_uploads_url?(url) absolute_url = Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(absolute_url, include_content_disposition: false) end return unless is_valid_image_url?(absolute_url) upload = Upload.get_from_url(absolute_url) if upload && upload.width && upload.width > 0 @size_cache[url] = [upload.width, upload.height] else @size_cache[url] = FastImage.size(absolute_url) end rescue Zlib::BufError, URI::Error, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError # FastImage.size raises BufError for some gifs, leave it. end def get_filename(upload, src) return File.basename(src) unless upload return upload.original_filename unless upload.original_filename =~ /\Ablob(\.png)?\z/i I18n.t("upload.pasted_image_filename") end def is_valid_image_url?(url) uri = URI.parse(url) %w[http https].include? uri.scheme rescue URI::Error end def add_large_image_placeholder!(img) url = img["src"] is_hyperlinked = is_a_hyperlink?(img) placeholder = create_node("div", "large-image-placeholder") img.add_next_sibling(placeholder) placeholder.add_child(img) a = create_link_node(nil, url, true) img.add_next_sibling(a) span = create_span_node("url", url) a.add_child(span) span.add_previous_sibling(create_icon_node("far-image")) span.add_next_sibling( create_span_node( "help", I18n.t( "upload.placeholders.too_large_humanized", max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size( SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes, ), ), ), ) # Only if the image is already linked if is_hyperlinked parent = placeholder.parent parent.add_next_sibling(placeholder) if parent.name == "a" && parent["href"].present? if url == parent["href"] parent.remove else parent["class"] = "link" a.add_previous_sibling(parent) lspan = create_span_node("url", parent["href"]) parent.add_child(lspan) lspan.add_previous_sibling(create_icon_node("link")) end end end img.remove true end def add_broken_image_placeholder!(img) img.name = "span" img.set_attribute("class", "broken-image") img.set_attribute("title", I18n.t("post.image_placeholder.broken")) img << "" img.remove_attribute("src") img.remove_attribute("width") img.remove_attribute("height") true end def add_blocked_hotlinked_image_placeholder!(el) el.name = "a" el.set_attribute("href", el[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR]) el.set_attribute("class", "blocked-hotlinked-placeholder") el.set_attribute("title", I18n.t("post.image_placeholder.blocked_hotlinked_title")) el << "" el << "#{CGI.escapeHTML(I18n.t("post.image_placeholder.blocked_hotlinked"))}" true end def add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholder!(el, src) placeholder = Nokogiri::XML::Node.new("a", el.document) placeholder.name = "a" placeholder.set_attribute("href", src) placeholder.set_attribute("class", "blocked-hotlinked-placeholder") placeholder.set_attribute("title", I18n.t("post.media_placeholder.blocked_hotlinked_title")) placeholder << "" placeholder << "#{CGI.escapeHTML(I18n.t("post.media_placeholder.blocked_hotlinked"))}" el.replace(placeholder) true end def oneboxed_images @doc.css(".onebox-body img, .onebox img, img.onebox") end def is_a_hyperlink?(img) parent = img.parent while parent return true if parent.name == "a" parent = parent.parent if parent.respond_to?(:parent) end false end def process_inline_onebox(element) inline_onebox = InlineOneboxer.lookup( element.attributes["href"].value, invalidate: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes], user_id: @model&.user_id, category_id: @category_id, ) if title = inline_onebox&.dig(:title) element.children = CGI.escapeHTML(title) element.add_class("inline-onebox") end remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element) end def remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element) element.remove_class("inline-onebox-loading") end def dirty? @previous_cooked != html end def html @doc.try(:to_html) end def create_link_node(klass, url, external = false) a = create_node("a", klass) a["href"] = url if external a["target"] = "_blank" a["rel"] = "nofollow noopener" end a end def create_icon_node(klass) icon = create_node("svg", "fa d-icon d-icon-#{klass} svg-icon") icon.set_attribute("aria-hidden", "true") icon << "" end def create_node(tag_name, klass) node = @doc.document.create_element(tag_name) node["class"] = klass if klass.present? @doc.add_child(node) node end def create_span_node(klass, content = nil) span = create_node("span", klass) span.content = content if content span end def each_responsive_ratio SiteSetting .responsive_post_image_sizes .split("|") .map(&:to_f) .sort .each { |r| yield r if r > 1 } end def is_svg?(img) path = begin URI(img["src"]).path rescue URI::Error nil end File.extname(path) == ".svg" if path end def convert_to_link!(img) w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i user_width, user_height = (w > 0 && h > 0 && [w, h]) || get_size_from_attributes(img) || get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes]) limit_size!(img) src = img["src"] return if src.blank? || is_a_hyperlink?(img) upload = Upload.get_from_url(src) original_width, original_height = nil if (upload.present?) original_width = upload.width || 0 original_height = upload.height || 0 else original_width, original_height = (get_size(src) || [0, 0]).map(&:to_i) if original_width == 0 || original_height == 0 Rails.logger.info "Can't reach '#{src}' to get its dimension." return end end if (upload.present? && upload.animated?) || src.match?(GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP) img.add_class("animated") end generate_thumbnail = original_width > SiteSetting.max_image_width || original_height > SiteSetting.max_image_height user_width, user_height = [original_width, original_height] if user_width.to_i <= 0 && user_height.to_i <= 0 width, height = user_width, user_height crop = SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop > 0 && width.to_f / height.to_f < SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop if crop width, height = ImageSizer.crop(width, height) img["width"], img["height"] = width, height else width, height = ImageSizer.resize(width, height) end if upload.present? if generate_thumbnail upload.create_thumbnail!(width, height, crop: crop) each_responsive_ratio do |ratio| resized_w = (width * ratio).to_i resized_h = (height * ratio).to_i if upload.width && resized_w <= upload.width upload.create_thumbnail!(resized_w, resized_h, crop: crop) end end end return if upload.animated? if img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").blank? && !img.classes.include?("onebox") add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop) end optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: crop) if generate_thumbnail end end def process_hotlinked_image(img) onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first # Skip hotlinked media processing if @post is not available (e.g., for chat messages) return true if @post.nil? @hotlinked_map ||= @post.post_hotlinked_media.preload(:upload).index_by(&:url) normalized_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(img["src"] || img[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR]) info = @hotlinked_map[normalized_src] still_an_image = true if info&.too_large? if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1 add_large_image_placeholder!(img) else img.remove end still_an_image = false elsif info&.download_failed? if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1 add_broken_image_placeholder!(img) else img.remove end still_an_image = false elsif info&.downloaded? && upload = info&.upload img["src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) img["data-dominant-color"] = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence img.delete(PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR) end still_an_image end def optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: false) w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first # note: optimize_urls cooks the src further after this thumbnail = upload.thumbnail(w, h) if thumbnail && thumbnail.filesize.to_i < upload.filesize img["src"] = thumbnail.url srcset = +"" # Skip srcset for onebox images. Because onebox thumbnails by default # are fairly small the width/height of the smallest thumbnail is likely larger # than what the onebox thumbnail size will be displayed at, so we shouldn't # need to upscale for retina devices if !onebox each_responsive_ratio do |ratio| resized_w = (w * ratio).to_i resized_h = (h * ratio).to_i if !cropped && upload.width && resized_w > upload.width cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x" elsif t = upload.thumbnail(resized_w, resized_h) cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(t.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x" end img[ "srcset" ] = "#{UrlHelper.cook_url(img["src"], secure: @should_secure_uploads)}#{srcset}" if srcset.present? end end else img["src"] = upload.url end if !@disable_dominant_color && (color = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence) img["data-dominant-color"] = color end end def add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop) return if original_width < MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH || original_height < MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT # first, create a div to hold our lightbox lightbox = create_node("div", LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS) img.add_next_sibling(lightbox) lightbox.add_child(img) # then, the link to our larger image src_url = Upload.secure_uploads_url?(img["src"]) ? upload&.url || img["src"] : img["src"] src = UrlHelper.cook_url(src_url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) a = create_link_node("lightbox", src) img.add_next_sibling(a) a["data-download-href"] = Discourse.store.download_url(upload) if upload a.add_child(img) # then, some overlay informations meta = create_node("div", "meta") img.add_next_sibling(meta) filename = get_filename(upload, img["src"]) informations = +"#{original_width}×#{original_height}" informations << " #{upload.human_filesize}" if upload a["title"] = img["title"] || img["alt"] || filename meta.add_child create_icon_node("far-image") meta.add_child create_span_node("filename", a["title"]) meta.add_child create_span_node("informations", informations) meta.add_child create_icon_node("discourse-expand") end end

file.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

##
# There are certain conditions with secure uploads when the security of
# uploads will need to change depending on the context they reside in.
#
# For example on these conditions:
#  * Topic category change
#  * Topic switching between PM and public topic
#  * Post moving between topics
#
# We need to go through all of the posts in that topic that
# own uploads via access_control_post_id, then for those uploads determine
# if they still need to be secure or not. For example an upload could be
# secure if it is in a PM, and then when the topic gets converted to a public
# topic the upload no longer needs to remain secure as it is no longer in
# a secure context.
class TopicUploadSecurityManager
  def initialize(topic)
    @topic = topic
  end

  def run
    rebaked_posts = []
    Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}")
    posts_owning_uploads.each do |post|
      Post.transaction do
        Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}")
        post.topic = @topic

        secure_status_did_change =
          post.owned_uploads_via_access_control.any? do |upload|
            # We already have the post preloaded so we may as well
            # attach it here to avoid another load in UploadSecurity
            # (which is called via update_secure_status)
            upload.access_control_post = post
            upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
          end

        if secure_status_did_change
          post.rebake!
          rebaked_posts << post
        end
        Rails.logger.debug(
          "Security updated & rebake complete in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
        )
      end
    end

    return rebaked_posts if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads

    # We only want to do this if secure uploads is enabled. If
    # the setting is turned on after a site has been running
    # already, we want to make sure that any post moves after
    # this are handled and upload secure statuses and ACLs
    # are updated appropriately, as well as setting the access control
    # post for secure uploads missing it.
    #
    # Examples (all after secure uploads is enabled):
    #
    #  -> a public topic is moved to a private category after
    #  -> a PM is converted to a public topic
    #  -> a public topic is converted to a PM
    #  -> a topic is moved from a private to a public category
    posts_with_unowned_uploads.each do |post|
      Post.transaction do
        Rails.logger.debug(
          "Setting upload access control posts in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
        )
        post.topic = @topic

        secure_status_did_change =
          post.uploads.any? do |upload|
            first_post_upload_appeared_in =
              upload.upload_references.where(target_type: "Post").first.target
            if first_post_upload_appeared_in == post
              upload.update(access_control_post: post)
              upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
            else
              false
            end
          end

        if secure_status_did_change
          post.rebake!
          rebaked_posts << post
        end
        Rails.logger.debug(
          "Completed changing access control posts #{secure_status_did_change ? "and rebaking" : ""} in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
        )
      end
    end

    Rails.logger.debug("Completed updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}!")
    rebaked_posts
  end

  private

  def posts_owning_uploads
    Post.where(topic_id: @topic.id, id: Upload.select(:access_control_post_id))
  end

  def posts_with_unowned_uploads
    Post.where(
      topic_id: @topic.id,
      id:
        UploadReference.where(
          target_type: "Post",
          upload: Upload.where(access_control_post_id: nil),
        ).select(:target_id),
    ).includes(:uploads)
  end
end

file.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

# Post processing that we can do after a post has already been cooked.
# For example, inserting the onebox content, or image sizes/thumbnails.

class CookedPostProcessor
  include CookedProcessorMixin

  attr_reader :cooking_options, :doc

  def initialize(post, opts = {})
    @dirty = false
    @opts = opts
    @post = post
    @model = post
    @previous_cooked = (@post.cooked || "").dup
    # NOTE: we re-cook the post here in order to prevent timing issues with edits
    # cf. https://meta.discourse.org/t/edit-of-rebaked-post-doesnt-show-in-html-only-in-raw/33815/6
    @cooking_options = post.cooking_options || opts[:cooking_options] || {}
    @cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
    @cooking_options = @cooking_options.symbolize_keys
    @should_secure_uploads = @post.should_secure_uploads?
    @category_id = @post&.topic&.category_id

    cooked = post.cook(post.raw, @cooking_options)
    @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
    @has_oneboxes = post.post_analyzer.found_oneboxes?
    @size_cache = {}

    @disable_dominant_color = !!opts[:disable_dominant_color]
    @omit_nofollow = post.omit_nofollow?
  end

  def post_process(new_post: false)
    DistributedMutex.synchronize("post_process_#{@post.id}", validity: 10.minutes) do
      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
      update_uploads_secure_status
      remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply if new_post
      post_process_oneboxes
      post_process_images
      post_process_videos
      add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
      post_process_quotes
      optimize_urls
      remove_user_ids
      update_post_image
      enforce_nofollow
      grant_badges
      @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
      nil
    end
  end

  def has_emoji?
    (@doc.css("img.emoji") - @doc.css(".quote img")).size > 0
  end

  def grant_badges
    return if @post.user.blank? || !Guardian.new.can_see?(@post)

    BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstEmoji), @post.user, post_id: @post.id) if has_emoji?
    if @has_oneboxes
      BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstOnebox), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
    end
    if @post.is_reply_by_email?
      BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstReplyByEmail), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
    end
  end

  def post_process_quotes
    @doc
      .css("aside.quote")
      .each do |q|
        post_number = q["data-post"]
        topic_id = q["data-topic"]
        if topic_id && post_number
          comparer = QuoteComparer.new(topic_id.to_i, post_number.to_i, q.css("blockquote").text)

          q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-post-not-found").strip if comparer.missing?
          q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-modified").strip if comparer.modified?
        end
      end
  end

  def update_uploads_secure_status
    @post.update_uploads_secure_status(source: "post processor")
  end

  def remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply
    return if !SiteSetting.remove_full_quote
    return if @post.post_number == 1
    return if @doc.xpath("aside[contains(@class, 'quote')]").size != 1

    previous =
      Post
        .where(
          "post_number < ? AND topic_id = ? AND post_type = ? AND NOT hidden",
          @post.post_number,
          @post.topic_id,
          Post.types[:regular],
        )
        .order("post_number DESC")
        .limit(1)
        .pluck(:cooked)
        .first

    return if previous.blank?

    previous_text = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(previous).text.strip
    quoted_text = @doc.css("aside.quote:first-child blockquote").first&.text&.strip || ""

    return if previous_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1') != quoted_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1')

    quote_regexp = %r{\A\s*\[quote.+\[/quote\]}im
    quoteless_raw = @post.raw.sub(quote_regexp, "").strip

    return if @post.raw.strip == quoteless_raw

    PostRevisor.new(@post).revise!(
      Discourse.system_user,
      { raw: quoteless_raw, edit_reason: I18n.t(:removed_direct_reply_full_quotes) },
      skip_validations: true,
      bypass_bump: true,
    )
  end

  def extract_images_for_post
    # all images with a src attribute
    @doc.css("img[src]") -
      # minus emojis
      @doc.css("img.emoji") -
      # minus images inside quotes
      @doc.css(".quote img") -
      # minus onebox site icons
      @doc.css("img.site-icon") -
      # minus onebox avatars
      @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar") - @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar-inline") -
      # minus github onebox profile images
      @doc.css(".onebox.githubfolder img")
  end

  def update_post_image
    upload = nil
    images = extract_images_for_post

    @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css("[data-thumbnail]")) do |url, path, sha1|
      upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
      break if upload
    end

    if upload.nil? # No specified thumbnail. Use any image:
      @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css(":not([data-thumbnail])")) do |url, path, sha1|
        upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
        break if upload
      end
    end

    if upload.present?
      @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id) # post
      if @post.is_first_post? # topic
        @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id)
        extra_sizes =
          ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.user_selectable.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
        @post.topic.generate_thumbnails!(extra_sizes: extra_sizes)
      end
    else
      @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil) if @post.image_upload_id
      if @post.topic.image_upload_id && @post.is_first_post?
        @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil)
      end
      nil
    end
  end

  def optimize_urls
    %w[href data-download-href].each do |selector|
      @doc.css("a[#{selector}]").each { |a| a[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(a[selector].to_s) }
    end

    %w[src].each do |selector|
      @doc
        .css("img[#{selector}], video[#{selector}]")
        .each do |img|
          custom_emoji = img["class"]&.include?("emoji-custom") && Emoji.custom?(img["title"])
          img[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(
            img[selector].to_s,
            secure: @should_secure_uploads && !custom_emoji,
          )
        end
    end
  end

  def remove_user_ids
    @doc
      .css("a[href]")
      .each do |a|
        uri =
          begin
            URI(a["href"])

    def download_url(upload)
      return unless upload
      "#{upload.short_path}?dl=1"
    end

    def path_for(upload)
      url = upload&.url
      FileStore::LocalStore.new.path_for(upload) if url && url[%r{\A/[^/]}]
    end

    def url_for(upload, force_download: false)
      if upload.secure? || force_download
        presigned_get_url(
          get_upload_key(upload),
          force_download: force_download,
          filename: upload.original_filename,
        )
      elsif SiteSetting.s3_use_cdn_url_for_all_uploads
        cdn_url(upload.url)
      else
        upload.url
      end
    end

    def cdn_url(url)
      return url if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
      schema = url[%r{\A(https?:)?//}, 1]
      folder = s3_bucket_folder_path.nil? ? "" : "#{s3_bucket_folder_path}/"
      url.sub(
        File.join("#{schema}#{absolute_base_url}", folder),
        File.join(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url, "/"),
      )
    end

    def signed_url_for_path(
      path,
      expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds,
      force_download: false,
      filename: nil,
      include_content_disposition:
    )
      key = path.sub(absolute_base_url + "/", "")

      presigned_get_url(
        key,
        expires_in:,
        force_download:,
        filename: include_content_disposition ? (filename || File.basename(path)) : nil,
      )
    end

    def signed_request_for_temporary_upload(
      file_name,
      expires_in: S3Helper::UPLOAD_URL_EXPIRES_AFTER_SECONDS,
      metadata: {}

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full audit.

---

# Upload Contract Audit — Discourse

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Discourse is a large Ruby on Rails (+ Ember.js frontend) community/forum platform. Multisite-aware (`RailsMultisite`), backed by Postgres + Redis + Sidekiq, with a heavy plugin system.

**Code layout (Rails-standard, server side):**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models (`upload.rb`, `optimized_image.rb`, `optimized_video.rb`, `upload_reference.rb`, `post.rb`, `user_avatar.rb`, etc.) + `app/models/concerns/` (shared mixins).
- `app/jobs/regular/` (enqueued) and `app/jobs/scheduled/` (cron) — Sidekiq jobs.
- `app/services/`, `app/serializers/`, `app/controllers/`, `app/helpers/`, `app/mailers/views`.
- `lib/` — the bulk of the domain logic that isn't a model: `lib/file_store/` (storage backends), `lib/upload_creator.rb`, `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/backup_restore/`, `lib/s3_*`, `lib/email/`, etc.
- `spec/` — RSpec tests mirroring `app/`+`lib/`; `spec/fabricators/` for fixtures.
- `plugins/` — first-party plugins; `lib/plugin/instance.rb` is the plugin API surface.

**The uploaded-file model.** `Upload` lives at `app/models/upload.rb:5`. It is the thing posts, avatars, themes, site settings, emails, and backups all reference. It is built on:
- `HasUrl` concern — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` (URL→record resolution).
- `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` (polymorphic join table; how everything "hangs off" an upload).
- `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`) and `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb:4`) — derived/variant records it spawns.
- `Discourse.store` (`lib/discourse.rb:958`) returning `FileStore::LocalStore` or `FileStore::S3Store` (subclasses of `FileStore::BaseStore`, `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7`) — the storage layer that resolves paths/URLs and physically stores/removes bytes.

---

## Step 2 — The Upload contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/upload.rb:5`.

**Shared behavior it pulls in (the concern):**
- `include HasUrl` — `app/models/upload.rb:7`; concern at `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3`. Provides class methods `extract_url`/`extract_sha1`/`get_from_url`/`get_from_urls` keyed off the class's `URL_REGEX` (`app/models/upload.rb:11`). This is the canonical "URL string → Upload record" resolver. `OptimizedImage` includes the same concern (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:4`) with its own `URL_REGEX` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:9`).
- `include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper` — `app/models/upload.rb:6` (for `human_filesize`, `app/models/upload.rb:484`).

**Associations other records hang off it (`app/models/upload.rb:17-34`):**
- `belongs_to :user` (`:17`); `belongs_to :access_control_post, class_name: "Post"` (`:18`, unscoped override at `:22`) — the security-context anchor.
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media, dependent: :destroy` (`:26`).
- `has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy` (`:27`) — variant records.
- `has_many :optimized_videos, dependent: :destroy` + `optimized_video_uploads through:` (`:28-29`).
- `has_many :user_uploads, dependent: :destroy` (`:30`).
- `has_many :upload_references, dependent: :destroy` and `has_many :posts, through: :upload_references` (`:31-32`) — **the central polymorphic dependency hub**.
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails` (`:33`); `has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify` (`:34`).

**The polymorphic reference contract:** `UploadReference` (`app/models/upload_reference.rb:3`) — `belongs_to :upload` + `belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true`; `ensure_exist!` (`:9`) is the idempotent sync API every target uses (see Step 4).

**Variant/derived records it spawns:**
- `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`), created via `Upload#get_optimized_image`/`create_thumbnail!` (`app/models/upload.rb:147,155`) → `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:35`).
- `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb:4`), spawned by `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` (`app/models/upload.rb:35`).

**Lifecycle hooks a teammate must know (these *are* the teardown contract today):**
- `before_destroy` nulls `UserProfile.card_background_upload_id`/`profile_background_upload_id` (`app/models/upload.rb:53-58`).
- `after_destroy` nulls `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar.gravatar_upload_id`/`custom_upload_id` (`app/models/upload.rb:60-64`).
- Overridden `#destroy` wraps in a transaction and calls `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` **before** the row delete (`app/models/upload.rb:212-217`) — physical file removal is coupled to row deletion.
- `validates_with UploadValidator` (`app/models/upload.rb:51`).

**Key contract surface (methods other code calls):** `url`/`to_s` (`:131`), `short_url`/`short_path`/`base62_sha1` (`:219,236,287`), `secure?` + `update_secure_status` (`:492`), `local?` (`:291`), `content`/`fix_dimensions!`/`dominant_color` (`:174,295,367`), `width/height/thumbnail_*` lazy getters (`:351-365`), and the class-level URL/sha1 resolvers `fetch_from` (`:86`), `secure_uploads_url?` (`:248`), `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`:259`), `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url` (`:266`), `sha1_from_short_url`/`sha1_from_long_url`/`sha1_from_short_path` (`:457-466`), `extract_upload_ids` (`:656`). Verification-status enum + scopes for S3 reconciliation (`:68-84`). Plugin callback registry: `add_unused_callback`/`add_in_use_callback` (`:96-118`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** changing `url`/sha1/`short_url` semantics ripples through `HasUrl` resolution, `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_*` (which *parses the URL back into a path*, `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124-154`), and every cooked-HTML resolver. Changing teardown means the `before/after_destroy` nulling, the in-transaction `store.remove_upload`, and all `dependent: :destroy` cascades (optimized images/videos, references, hotlinked media, user_uploads) must be preserved or consciously re-homed.

---

## Step 3 — How an upload is created and processed

**Creation entry point:** `UploadCreator#create_for(user_id)` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59` (constructed at `:44`). Runs **inline** (synchronous, inside an HTTP request or job):
1. Under a `DistributedMutex` per `user_#{id}_#{filename}` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:91`).
2. Image pipeline (inline): HEIF→JPEG (`:93`), `extract_image_info!` (`:99`), SVG sanitize / ico→png / png→jpg / orient / crop / optimize / downsize (`:102-113`).
3. Computes `sha1` (`:121`) or a fake unique hash for secure uploads (`:122`); dedup lookup by sha1 unless secure (`:132-148`).
4. Builds the `Upload` row, sets `sha1`/`original_sha1`/`extension`/dimensions, `calculate_dominant_color!` (inline, `:214`), security via `UploadSecurity` (`:219-224`), fires `DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, ...)` (`:232`) for plugin validation.
5. **Storage (inline):** `Discourse.store.store_upload(f, @upload)` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:259`) → `BaseStore#store_upload` computes path via `get_path_for_upload` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:12,124`) → backend `store_file`. For S3 that's `S3Store#store_upload` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:32`); for "too big" external uploads it `move_existing_stored_upload` instead (`:48`, called at upload_creator `:253`).
6. Sets `@upload.url` from the store result and `save!` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:265-267`).

**Processing that fans out:**
- **Avatar thumbnails — async:** `Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, ...)` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:276`) → `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:7`.
- **Video conversion — async:** `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` (`app/models/upload.rb:35,690`) → `Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, ...)` → `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb`, status polled by `app/jobs/regular/check_video_conversion_status.rb`, adapters in `app/services/video_conversion/`.
- **Optimized images (thumbnails) — inline during cooking:** `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:35`) under `DistributedMutex` (`:11`); invoked from `CookedProcessorMixin#convert_to_link!` → `upload.create_thumbnail!` (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:463,470`).
- **Post processing — async, then inline within the job:** post creation enqueues `:process_post` via `PostJobsEnqueuer#trigger_post_post_process` (`lib/post_jobs_enqueuer.rb:40`) → `CookedPostProcessor#post_process` (`lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34`), which runs `update_uploads_secure_status` (`:37,86`), `post_process_images`/`post_process_videos` (`:41-42`), thumbnailing + lightboxing (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:128,414`), `optimize_urls` (`:44,176`), `update_post_image` (`:46,143`), and `link_post_uploads` (`:49`).
- **Hotlinked images — async:** `Jobs.enqueue(:pull_hotlinked_images, ...)` → `app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb:7` downloads remote images, creates Uploads, records `PostHotlinkedMedia`, then rebakes.
- **Remote storage / secure-status / ACL — async + inline:** `update_secure_status` (`app/models/upload.rb:492`) calls `Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control` inline when external (`:503`); bulk ACL sync is async via `app/jobs/regular/sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb:9`. Migration to new URL scheme runs async hourly: `app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb:8` → `Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme` (`app/models/upload.rb:533`).

---

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Upload contract (grouped, exhaustive)

### A. Core model / shared pieces (the contract itself)
1. `app/models/upload.rb:5` — the model.
2. `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` — URL→record resolver mixed into Upload.
3. `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` — polymorphic join + `ensure_exist!`.
4. `app/models/optimized_image.rb:3` — variant; `belongs_to :upload` (`:5`), `create_for` (`:35`).
5. `app/models/optimized_video.rb:4` — variant; `belongs_to :upload` (`:4`), `optimized_upload_id` → uploads (`:67`).
6. `app/models/user_upload.rb` — `has_many :user_uploads` join.
7. `app/models/external_upload_stub.rb` — staging record before an Upload exists.
8. `app/models/post_hotlinked_media.rb` — `belongs_to :upload`, per-post hotlink map.
9. `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb` — `belongs_to :upload`/`optimized_image`.
10. `lib/upload_creator.rb:59` — builds Uploads.
11. `lib/upload_security.rb:27` — computes `secure?`.
12. `lib/upload_markdown.rb` — `Upload#to_markdown` (`app/models/upload.rb:135`).
13. `lib/upload_recovery.rb:3` — re-creates missing post Uploads from store/tombstone.

### B. Post rendering & cooking
14. `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34` — orchestrates post processing; `Upload.fetch_from` (`:148,154`), `update_post_image` (`:143`), `post_process_videos` `Upload.get_from_url` (`:232`).
15. `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb` — `get_size`/`convert_to_link!`/`optimize_image!`/`process_hotlinked_image`/`add_lightbox!` all resolve and thumbnail Uploads (`:214,425,463,514,575,581`).
16. `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:3` — `link_post_uploads` (`:6`) + `each_upload_url` (`:56`); sets `access_control_post_id` (`:48-52`). Included by `Post` (`app/models/post.rb:13`) and `PostLocalization` (`app/models/post_localization.rb:5`).
17. `app/models/post.rb` — `has_many :uploads, through: :upload_references` (`:44`), `belongs_to :image_upload` (`:68`), `should_secure_uploads?` (`:588`), `update_uploads_secure_status` (`:1094`), `owned_uploads_via_access_control` (`:1168`), `image_url` via `UrlHelper.cook_url` (`:1173`).
18. `app/models/post_localization.rb:5` — localized cooked content references uploads.
19. `lib/localized_cooked_post_processor.rb` — post processing for localized posts.
20. `lib/pretty_text.rb` & `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb` — markdown→HTML; upload short-url lookup.
21. `app/services/inline_uploads.rb` — rewrites raw markdown upload URLs ↔ short URLs.
22. `lib/excerpt_parser.rb` / `lib/email_cook.rb` — consume cooked HTML containing upload URLs (indirect).
23. `lib/quote_rewriter.rb` — rewrites quotes (cooked HTML, indirect).

### C. Storage & CDN
24. `lib/discourse.rb:958` — `Discourse.store` factory.
25. `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7` — `store_upload`/`store_optimized_image`/`remove_upload`/`get_path_for_upload`/`get_path_for_optimized_image`/`download`/`path_for` — **parses `upload.url` back to a path** (`:124-154`).
26. `lib/file_store/local_store.rb` — local backend.
27. `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:8` — S3 backend; `url_for`/`cdn_url`/`signed_url_for_path`/`has_been_uploaded?`/`update_upload_access_control` (`:142,208,218,232,242`).
28. `lib/file_store/to_s3_migration.rb` — local→S3 migration.
29. `lib/s3_helper.rb` — low-level S3 ops.
30. `lib/s3_inventory.rb:32` — reconciles Upload rows vs S3 inventory; mutates `verification_status`/`etag` (`:99-135`).
31. `lib/s3_cors_rulesets.rb` — S3 CORS for direct uploads.
32. `lib/url_helper.rb:128` — `cook_url`/`secure_proxy_without_cdn`/`absolute` — final URL the browser sees.
33. `lib/global_path.rb:12` — `upload_cdn_path`/`full_cdn_url` (`:26`).
34. `lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6` — `upload_from_path_and_extension`/`upload_from_full_url`/`check_secure_upload_permission`.
35. `lib/external_upload_helpers.rb` — direct-to-S3 multipart upload flow.
36. `app/services/external_upload_manager.rb` — promotes `ExternalUploadStub` → `Upload`.
37. `app/services/handle_chunk_upload.rb` — chunked upload assembly.
38. `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb` — upload create/show/`show_secure` endpoints.
39. `app/services/problem_check/s3_cdn.rb` & `s3_upload_config.rb` — admin health checks on upload storage config.
40. `lib/final_destination/ssrf_detector.rb` — guards downloads of remote/upload URLs.
41. `lib/disk_space.rb` — reports uploads dir usage.

### D. Background jobs
42. `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:7` — avatar optimized images.
43. `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb` — spawns OptimizedVideo.
44. `app/jobs/regular/check_video_conversion_status.rb` — polls conversion.
45. `app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb:7` — downloads → creates Uploads + PostHotlinkedMedia.
46. `app/jobs/regular/pull_user_profile_hotlinked_images.rb` — same for profiles.
47. `app/jobs/regular/sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb:9` — bulk S3 ACL updates.
48. `app/jobs/regular/update_post_uploads_secure_status.rb:9` — per-post `update_secure_status`.
49. `app/jobs/regular/update_topic_upload_security.rb` — wraps `TopicUploadSecurityManager`.
50. `app/jobs/regular/rebake_posts_for_upload.rb:6` — rebakes posts of an upload.
51. `app/jobs/regular/rebake_custom_emoji_posts.rb` — rebakes emoji posts.
52. `app/jobs/regular/generate_topic_thumbnails.rb` — topic thumbnails from `image_upload`.
53. `app/jobs/regular/download_avatar_from_url.rb` — creates avatar Upload.
54. `app/jobs/regular/update_gravatar.rb` — gravatar Upload via UserAvatar.
55. `app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:7` — **destroys unreferenced uploads** (uses `unused_callbacks`, `with_no_non_post_relations`).
56. `app/jobs/scheduled/purge_deleted_uploads.rb:7` — `store.purge_tombstone`.
57. `app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb:8` — `Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme`; destroys stale optimized images.
58. `app/jobs/scheduled/ensure_s3_uploads_existence.rb:24` — runs `S3Inventory`.
59. `app/jobs/scheduled/update_animated_uploads.rb` — sets `animated`.
60. `app/jobs/scheduled/create_missing_avatars.rb` — backfills avatar optimized images.

### E. Email
61. `lib/email/sender.rb:372` — attaches `post.uploads` / `optimized_images`, reads via `store.path_for`/`download!` (`:389-392`), `should_attach_image?` checks `upload.secure?` (`:415`).
62. `lib/email/styles.rb:361` — `stripped_upload_sha_map`/`stripped_secure_image_uploads`/`inline_secure_images` (`:373,378`), `Upload.sha1_from_long_url` (`:367`).
63. `lib/email/receiver.rb:1373` — `add_attachments` creates Uploads from mail; dedup via `UploadReference`/`original_sha1` (`:1377-1381`).
64. `app/helpers/email_helper.rb` — email view helpers referencing upload/avatar URLs.

### F. Backup & restore
65. `lib/backup_restore/uploads_restorer.rb:6` — restores upload files; `remap_uploads`/`remap_s3` rewrite upload URLs (`:88,103`), `generate_optimized_images` (`:51`).
66. `lib/backup_restore/creator.rb` — includes uploads in backup archive.
67. `app/controllers/admin/backups_controller.rb` — backup upload/download endpoints (uses Upload/store).
68. `app/jobs/regular/create_backup.rb`, `backup_chunks_merger.rb`, `download_backup_email.rb` — backup file handling (`for_export` uploads).
69. `app/jobs/scheduled/schedule_backup.rb` / `drop_backup_schema.rb` — backup lifecycle.

### G. Theming & site assets
70. `app/models/theme_field.rb:10` — `belongs_to :upload`; `ensure_exist!` (`:29`), SVG sprite handling (`:668-692`).
71. `app/models/theme.rb` — theme upload fields/`upload_references`.
72. `app/models/theme_setting.rb:6` — upload-typed theme settings (`ensure_exist!` `:23,44`).
73. `app/models/theme_site_setting.rb:13` — upload-typed (`:18,27`).
74. `app/models/theme_svg_sprite.rb:18` — `upload_id` not null.
75. `app/models/remote_theme.rb` — imports theme uploads.
76. `lib/theme_settings_manager/upload.rb:3` — resolves theme upload `cdn_url` (`:12,30`).
77. `lib/stylesheet/manager/builder.rb` — embeds upload/asset URLs in compiled CSS.
78. `lib/site_icon_manager.rb` — favicon/app icons from site-setting Uploads → optimized images.
79. `app/controllers/metadata_controller.rb` & `static_controller.rb` — manifest/opengraph/asset image URLs from Uploads.
80. `lib/svg_sprite.rb` (theme sprite) / `app/serializers/theme_field_serializer.rb` — theme upload serialization.

### H. User profiles & avatars
81. `app/models/user.rb:201` — `uploaded_avatar_id`; `ensure_exist!`.
82. `app/models/user_avatar.rb:7` — `custom_upload_id`/`gravatar_upload_id`, `ensure_exist!` (`:12`), optimized-image bookkeeping (`:194`).
83. `app/models/user_profile.rb:12` — `card_background_upload_id`/`profile_background_upload_id`, `ensure_exist!` (`:40`).
84. `app/models/user_export.rb:5` — `belongs_to :upload, dependent: :destroy`, `ensure_exist!` (`:12`).
85. `app/controllers/user_avatars_controller.rb` — serves avatar images (optimized).
86. `app/controllers/users_controller.rb` — avatar/card/profile background upload endpoints.
87. `app/services/user_updater.rb` — sets profile/avatar upload ids.
88. `app/services/user_merger.rb` — re-points upload ownership/references on merge.
89. `lib/letter_avatar.rb` — generates fallback avatars (store paths).

## Step 5 — How identity & storage location are guarded

**"Is this a real, current upload?" guards:**
- **By sha1/url:** `Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` (`app/models/upload.rb:86`) — sha1 lookup first, else `HasUrl.get_from_url` (`app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:21`), which extracts sha1 from the path via `URL_REGEX`/`extract_sha1` and only matches a 40-char sha1 (`has_url.rb:11-18,41-47`). `get_from_urls` batches it (`has_url.rb:49`).
- **Short-URL/long-URL identity:** `sha1_from_short_url`/`sha1_from_short_path`/`sha1_from_long_url`/`sha1_from_base62_encoded` (`app/models/upload.rb:457-478`) — bounded by `MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH`/`SHA1_LENGTH`. Used in cooked HTML scanning (`has_post_upload_references.rb:99,113,138-143`) and recovery.
- **Secure-URL recognition:** `Upload.secure_uploads_url?` (`app/models/upload.rb:248`) checks the URL routes to `uploads#show_secure` and is supported media; `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url` (`:266`) and `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`:259`) convert.
- **Permission guard at the secure endpoint:** `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#upload_from_full_url`/`upload_from_path_and_extension`/`check_secure_upload_permission` (`lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6,13,17`).
- **Hotlinked-media guard (post processing):** `process_hotlinked_image` keys off `@post.post_hotlinked_media` map and `downloaded?`/`too_large?`/`download_failed?` before swapping in `upload.url` (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:485-518`).
- **Security context:** `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` (`lib/upload_security.rb:75`) anchored on `access_control_post` (`:87,180`); `Post#should_secure_uploads?` (`app/models/post.rb:588`); topic-level re-evaluation in `TopicUploadSecurityManager#run` (`lib/topic_upload_security_manager.rb:23`) using `owned_uploads_via_access_control` and the first `Post` reference (`:32,73-76`).
- **S3 existence/identity reconciliation:** `S3Inventory.backfill_etags_and_list_missing` (`lib/s3_inventory.rb:42`) compares `etag` vs inventory, setting `verification_status` to `verified`/`invalid_etag`/`s3_file_missing_confirmed` (`:99-135`); scopes `without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status` / `with_invalid_etag_verification_status` and `mark_invalid_s3_uploads_as_missing` (`app/models/upload.rb:68-94`). Recovery: `UploadRecovery#ensure_upload!` re-checks `upload.sha1 != sha1` (`lib/upload_recovery.rb:67-75`).

**Storage location / URL resolvers (where path/URL is derived):**
- **Path from URL (canonical):** `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload`/`get_path_for_optimized_image` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124,142`) — prefer parsing `upload.url` with `UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX`/`OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX` (`:8-9`), else compute from `get_path_for(type,id,sha,ext)` (`:118`) using `get_depth_for(id)` (`:226`).
- **Local path:** `LocalStore#path_for`; **S3 path:** `S3Store#path_for` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:213`, only for legacy local-style URLs).
- **Public/CDN URL:** `S3Store#url_for`/`cdn_url`/`download_url` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:208,218,232`); `UrlHelper.cook_url` (`lib/url_helper.rb:128`) + `secure_proxy_without_cdn` (`:88`); `GlobalPath#upload_cdn_path`/`full_cdn_url` (`lib/global_path.rb:12,26`).
- **Signed URLs (secure):** `S3Store#signed_url_for_path`/`presigned_get_url` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:242`); `Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`app/models/upload.rb:259`).
- **Download for offline ops:** `BaseStore#perform_download` chooses signed-vs-cdn by `secure?` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:184-201`), cached under `tmp/download_cache/` keyed by sha1; consumed by `Upload#content`/`fix_dimensions!`/`calculate_dominant_color!` (`app/models/upload.rb:174,295,377`), `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:66-71`), and `Email::Sender` (`lib/email/sender.rb:389-392`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

**🔴 Critical — silent data loss / broken teardown:**
1. **`Upload#destroy` + lifecycle hooks** (`app/models/upload.rb:53-64,212-217`). The in-transaction `store.remove_upload` and the manual FK-nulling (UserProfile/User/UserAvatar) are *not* DB-level cascades — if you change teardown, orphaned files, dangling `uploaded_avatar_id`, or files deleted while still referenced are all possible. **Re-verify:** all `dependent:` cascades (optimized images/videos, references, hotlinked media, user_uploads) + the four nulled columns.
2. **`clean_up_uploads` job** (`app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:7`) + `with_no_non_post_relations` (`app/models/upload.rb:120`) + plugin `unused_callbacks` (`:96`). A change to what "referenced" means here can **mass-delete in-use uploads hourly**. Highest pager risk.
3. **`FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_*`** (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124-154`). Path is *reverse-derived from `url`*. Any change to URL shape/sha1/extension silently changes where files are read/written/removed → 404s and failed deletes across local + S3.

**🟠 High — broken rendering/links at scale:**
4. **`HasUrl` resolvers + `fetch_from`** (`has_url.rb`, `upload.rb:86`) — feed cooking, email, recovery, S3 inventory. Break these and uploads in posts stop resolving.
5. **Cooked-HTML pipeline** (`lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `cooked_processor_mixin.rb`, `has_post_upload_references.rb`) — thumbnails, lightboxes, srcset, `link_post_uploads`, `access_control_post_id` assignment. Requires mass rebake to fix if URL semantics change.
6. **`UrlHelper.cook_url` / `S3Store#url_for`/`cdn_url` / `GlobalPath`** — every browser-facing URL. Regressions hit all images site-wide.
7. **Secure uploads chain** (`upload_security.rb`, `topic_upload_security_manager.rb`, `update_secure_status` `upload.rb:492`, `uploads_controller#show_secure`, `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb`) — getting `secure?` wrong leaks private files or 404s legit ones.
8. **S3 reconciliation** (`s3_inventory.rb`, `verification_status` enum/scopes) — wrong status can mark live files missing (feeds critical risk #2's deletion candidates).

**🟡 Medium — feature-scoped:**
9. Variant spawn/teardown: `OptimizedImage.create_for`/`#destroy` (`optimized_image.rb:35,153`), `OptimizedVideo` + `convert_video` job.
10. Email embedding (`email/sender.rb:372`, `email/styles.rb`) — broken inline images / oversized attachments.
11. Backup/restore remapping (`uploads_restorer.rb:88-127`) — restores onto a new URL scheme; must track any URL change.
12. Avatars/profiles (`user_avatar.rb`, `user_profile.rb`, `user.rb`, avatar jobs/controllers) — protected by the `after_destroy` nulling above.
13. Theme/site assets (`theme_field.rb`, `theme_svg_sprite.rb`, `site_icon_manager.rb`, `stylesheet/manager/builder.rb`, `theme_settings_manager/upload.rb`).
14. `pull_hotlinked_images` + `post_hotlinked_media` (`cooked_processor_mixin.rb:485`).

**🟢 Lower — read/serialize only, but review for URL-shape changes:**
15. Serializers in Step 4-K; site-setting/plugin surface (Step 4-J); `migrate_upload_scheme` (already URL-scheme-aware); rake tasks.

**Must re-verify after the change (matrix):** post cook→render→email; avatar upload→thumbnail→display; secure-upload toggle→ACL sync→show_secure; backup→restore→remap; S3 inventory→verification→clean_up_uploads (ensure no live deletes); video convert; theme/site-icon assets.

---

# Upload Contract Audit — Discourse

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Discourse is a large Ruby on Rails (+ Ember.js frontend) community/forum platform. Multisite-aware (`RailsMultisite`), backed by Postgres + Redis + Sidekiq, with a heavy plugin system.

**Code layout (Rails-standard, server side):**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models (`upload.rb`, `optimized_image.rb`, `optimized_video.rb`, `upload_reference.rb`, `post.rb`, `user_avatar.rb`, etc.) + `app/models/concerns/` (shared mixins).
- `app/jobs/regular/` (enqueued) and `app/jobs/scheduled/` (cron) — Sidekiq jobs.
- `app/services/`, `app/serializers/`, `app/controllers/`, `app/helpers/`, `app/mailers/views`.
- `lib/` — the bulk of the domain logic that isn't a model: `lib/file_store/` (storage backends), `lib/upload_creator.rb`, `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/backup_restore/`, `lib/s3_*`, `lib/email/`, etc.
- `spec/` — RSpec tests mirroring `app/`+`lib/`; `spec/fabricators/` for fixtures.
- `plugins/` — first-party plugins; `lib/plugin/instance.rb` is the plugin API surface.

**The uploaded-file model.** `Upload` lives at `app/models/upload.rb:5`. It is the thing posts, avatars, themes, site settings, emails, and backups all reference. It is built on:
- `HasUrl` concern — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` (URL→record resolution).
- `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` (polymorphic join table; how everything "hangs off" an upload).
- `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`) and `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb:4`) — derived/variant records it spawns.
- `Discourse.store` (`lib/discourse.rb:958`) returning `FileStore::LocalStore` or `FileStore::S3Store` (subclasses of `FileStore::BaseStore`, `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7`) — the storage layer that resolves paths/URLs and physically stores/removes bytes.

---

## Step 2 — The Upload contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/upload.rb:5`.

**Shared behavior it pulls in (the concern):**
- `include HasUrl` — `app/models/upload.rb:7`; concern at `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3`. Provides class methods `extract_url`/`extract_sha1`/`get_from_url`/`get_from_urls` keyed off the class's `URL_REGEX` (`app/models/upload.rb:11`). This is the canonical "URL string → Upload record" resolver. `OptimizedImage` includes the same concern (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:4`) with its own `URL_REGEX` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:9`).
- `include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper` — `app/models/upload.rb:6` (for `human_filesize`, `app/models/upload.rb:484`).

**Associations other records hang off it (`app/models/upload.rb:17-34`):**
- `belongs_to :user` (`:17`); `belongs_to :access_control_post, class_name: "Post"` (`:18`, unscoped override at `:22`) — the security-context anchor.
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media, dependent: :destroy` (`:26`).
- `has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy` (`:27`) — variant records.
- `has_many :optimized_videos, dependent: :destroy` + `optimized_video_uploads through:` (`:28-29`).
- `has_many :user_uploads, dependent: :destroy` (`:30`).
- `has_many :upload_references, dependent: :destroy` and `has_many :posts, through: :upload_references` (`:31-32`) — **the central polymorphic dependency hub**.
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails` (`:33`); `has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify` (`:34`).

**The polymorphic reference contract:** `UploadReference` (`app/models/upload_reference.rb:3`) — `belongs_to :upload` + `belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true`; `ensure_exist!` (`:9`) is the idempotent sync API every target uses (see Step 4).

**Variant/derived records it spawns:**
- `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`), created via `Upload#get_optimized_image`/`create_thumbnail!` (`app/models/upload.rb:147,155`) → `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:35`).
- `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb:4`), spawned by `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` (`app/models/upload.rb:35`).

**Lifecycle hooks a teammate must know (these *are* the teardown contract today):**
- `before_destroy` nulls `UserProfile.card_background_upload_id`/`profile_background_upload_id` (`app/models/upload.rb:53-58`).
- `after_destroy` nulls `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar.gravatar_upload_id`/`custom_upload_id` (`app/models/upload.rb:60-64`).
- Overridden `#destroy` wraps in a transaction and calls `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` **before** the row delete (`app/models/upload.rb:212-217`) — physical file removal is coupled to row deletion.
- `validates_with UploadValidator` (`app/models/upload.rb:51`).

**Key contract surface (methods other code calls):** `url`/`to_s` (`:131`), `short_url`/`short_path`/`base62_sha1` (`:219,236,287`), `secure?` + `update_secure_status` (`:492`), `local?` (`:291`), `content`/`fix_dimensions!`/`dominant_color` (`:174,295,367`), `width/height/thumbnail_*` lazy getters (`:351-365`), and the class-level URL/sha1 resolvers `fetch_from` (`:86`), `secure_uploads_url?` (`:248`), `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`:259`), `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url` (`:266`), `sha1_from_short_url`/`sha1_from_long_url`/`sha1_from_short_path` (`:457-466`), `extract_upload_ids` (`:656`). Verification-status enum + scopes for S3 reconciliation (`:68-84`). Plugin callback registry: `add_unused_callback`/`add_in_use_callback` (`:96-118`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** changing `url`/sha1/`short_url` semantics ripples through `HasUrl` resolution, `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_*` (which *parses the URL back into a path*, `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124-154`), and every cooked-HTML resolver. Changing teardown means the `before/after_destroy` nulling, the in-transaction `store.remove_upload`, and all `dependent: :destroy` cascades (optimized images/videos, references, hotlinked media, user_uploads) must be preserved or consciously re-homed.

---

## Step 3 — How an upload is created and processed

**Creation entry point:** `UploadCreator#create_for(user_id)` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59` (constructed at `:44`). Runs **inline** (synchronous, inside an HTTP request or job):
1. Under a `DistributedMutex` per `user_#{id}_#{filename}` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:91`).
2. Image pipeline (inline): HEIF→JPEG (`:93`), `extract_image_info!` (`:99`), SVG sanitize / ico→png / png→jpg / orient / crop / optimize / downsize (`:102-113`).
3. Computes `sha1` (`:121`) or a fake unique hash for secure uploads (`:122`); dedup lookup by sha1 unless secure (`:132-148`).
4. Builds the `Upload` row, sets `sha1`/`original_sha1`/`extension`/dimensions, `calculate_dominant_color!` (inline, `:214`), security via `UploadSecurity` (`:219-224`), fires `DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, ...)` (`:232`) for plugin validation.
5. **Storage (inline):** `Discourse.store.store_upload(f, @upload)` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:259`) → `BaseStore#store_upload` computes path via `get_path_for_upload` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:12,124`) → backend `store_file`. For S3 that's `S3Store#store_upload` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:32`); for "too big" external uploads it `move_existing_stored_upload` instead (`:48`, called at upload_creator `:253`).
6. Sets `@upload.url` from the store result and `save!` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:265-267`).

**Processing that fans out:**
- **Avatar thumbnails — async:** `Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, ...)` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:276`) → `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:7`.
- **Video conversion — async:** `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` (`app/models/upload.rb:35,690`) → `Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, ...)` → `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb`, status polled by `app/jobs/regular/check_video_conversion_status.rb`, adapters in `app/services/video_conversion/`.
- **Optimized images (thumbnails) — inline during cooking:** `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:35`) under `DistributedMutex` (`:11`); invoked from `CookedProcessorMixin#convert_to_link!` → `upload.create_thumbnail!` (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:463,470`).
- **Post processing — async, then inline within the job:** post creation enqueues `:process_post` via `PostJobsEnqueuer#trigger_post_post_process` (`lib/post_jobs_enqueuer.rb:40`) → `CookedPostProcessor#post_process` (`lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34`), which runs `update_uploads_secure_status` (`:37,86`), `post_process_images`/`post_process_videos` (`:41-42`), thumbnailing + lightboxing (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:128,414`), `optimize_urls` (`:44,176`), `update_post_image` (`:46,143`), and `link_post_uploads` (`:49`).
- **Hotlinked images — async:** `Jobs.enqueue(:pull_hotlinked_images, ...)` → `app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb:7` downloads remote images, creates Uploads, records `PostHotlinkedMedia`, then rebakes.
- **Remote storage / secure-status / ACL — async + inline:** `update_secure_status` (`app/models/upload.rb:492`) calls `Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control` inline when external (`:503`); bulk ACL sync is async via `app/jobs/regular/sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb:9`. Migration to new URL scheme runs async hourly: `app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb:8` → `Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme` (`app/models/upload.rb:533`).

---

## Step 5 — How identity & storage location are guarded

**"Is this a real, current upload?" guards:**
- **By sha1/url:** `Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` (`app/models/upload.rb:86`) — sha1 lookup first, else `HasUrl.get_from_url` (`app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:21`), which extracts sha1 from the path via `URL_REGEX`/`extract_sha1` and only matches a 40-char sha1 (`has_url.rb:11-18,41-47`). `get_from_urls` batches it (`has_url.rb:49`).
- **Short-URL/long-URL identity:** `sha1_from_short_url`/`sha1_from_short_path`/`sha1_from_long_url`/`sha1_from_base62_encoded` (`app/models/upload.rb:457-478`) — bounded by `MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH`/`SHA1_LENGTH`. Used in cooked HTML scanning (`has_post_upload_references.rb:99,113,138-143`) and recovery.
- **Secure-URL recognition:** `Upload.secure_uploads_url?` (`app/models/upload.rb:248`) checks the URL routes to `uploads#show_secure` and is supported media; `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url` (`:266`) and `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`:259`) convert.
- **Permission guard at the secure endpoint:** `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#upload_from_full_url`/`upload_from_path_and_extension`/`check_secure_upload_permission` (`lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6,13,17`).
- **Hotlinked-media guard (post processing):** `process_hotlinked_image` keys off `@post.post_hotlinked_media` map and `downloaded?`/`too_large?`/`download_failed?` before swapping in `upload.url` (`lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:485-518`).
- **Security context:** `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` (`lib/upload_security.rb:75`) anchored on `access_control_post` (`:87,180`); `Post#should_secure_uploads?` (`app/models/post.rb:588`); topic-level re-evaluation in `TopicUploadSecurityManager#run` (`lib/topic_upload_security_manager.rb:23`) using `owned_uploads_via_access_control` and the first `Post` reference (`:32,73-76`).
- **S3 existence/identity reconciliation:** `S3Inventory.backfill_etags_and_list_missing` (`lib/s3_inventory.rb:42`) compares `etag` vs inventory, setting `verification_status` to `verified`/`invalid_etag`/`s3_file_missing_confirmed` (`:99-135`); scopes `without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status` / `with_invalid_etag_verification_status` and `mark_invalid_s3_uploads_as_missing` (`app/models/upload.rb:68-94`). Recovery: `UploadRecovery#ensure_upload!` re-checks `upload.sha1 != sha1` (`lib/upload_recovery.rb:67-75`).

**Storage location / URL resolvers (where path/URL is derived):**
- **Path from URL (canonical):** `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload`/`get_path_for_optimized_image` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124,142`) — prefer parsing `upload.url` with `UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX`/`OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX` (`:8-9`), else compute from `get_path_for(type,id,sha,ext)` (`:118`) using `get_depth_for(id)` (`:226`).
- **Local path:** `LocalStore#path_for`; **S3 path:** `S3Store#path_for` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:213`, only for legacy local-style URLs).
- **Public/CDN URL:** `S3Store#url_for`/`cdn_url`/`download_url` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:208,218,232`); `UrlHelper.cook_url` (`lib/url_helper.rb:128`) + `secure_proxy_without_cdn` (`:88`); `GlobalPath#upload_cdn_path`/`full_cdn_url` (`lib/global_path.rb:12,26`).
- **Signed URLs (secure):** `S3Store#signed_url_for_path`/`presigned_get_url` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:242`); `Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` (`app/models/upload.rb:259`).
- **Download for offline ops:** `BaseStore#perform_download` chooses signed-vs-cdn by `secure?` (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:184-201`), cached under `tmp/download_cache/` keyed by sha1; consumed by `Upload#content`/`fix_dimensions!`/`calculate_dominant_color!` (`app/models/upload.rb:174,295,377`), `OptimizedImage.create_for` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:66-71`), and `Email::Sender` (`lib/email/sender.rb:389-392`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

**🔴 Critical — silent data loss / broken teardown:**
1. **`Upload#destroy` + lifecycle hooks** (`app/models/upload.rb:53-64,212-217`). The in-transaction `store.remove_upload` and the manual FK-nulling (UserProfile/User/UserAvatar) are *not* DB-level cascades — if you change teardown, orphaned files, dangling `uploaded_avatar_id`, or files deleted while still referenced are all possible. **Re-verify:** all `dependent:` cascades (optimized images/videos, references, hotlinked media, user_uploads) + the four nulled columns.
2. **`clean_up_uploads` job** (`app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:7`) + `with_no_non_post_relations` (`app/models/upload.rb:120`) + plugin `unused_callbacks` (`:96`). A change to what "referenced" means here can **mass-delete in-use uploads hourly**. Highest pager risk.
3. **`FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_*`** (`lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124-154`). Path is *reverse-derived from `url`*. Any change to URL shape/sha1/extension silently changes where files are read/written/removed → 404s and failed deletes across local + S3.

**🟠 High — broken rendering/links at scale:**
4. **`HasUrl` resolvers + `fetch_from`** (`has_url.rb`, `upload.rb:86`) — feed cooking, email, recovery, S3 inventory. Break these and uploads in posts stop resolving.
5. **Cooked-HTML pipeline** (`lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `cooked_processor_mixin.rb`, `has_post_upload_references.rb`) — thumbnails, lightboxes, srcset, `link_post_uploads`, `access_control_post_id` assignment. Requires mass rebake to fix if URL semantics change.
6. **`UrlHelper.cook_url` / `S3Store#url_for`/`cdn_url` / `GlobalPath`** — every browser-facing URL. Regressions hit all images site-wide.
7. **Secure uploads chain** (`upload_security.rb`, `topic_upload_security_manager.rb`, `update_secure_status` `upload.rb:492`, `uploads_controller#show_secure`, `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb`) — getting `secure?` wrong leaks private files or 404s legit ones.
8. **S3 reconciliation** (`s3_inventory.rb`, `verification_status` enum/scopes) — wrong status can mark live files missing (feeds critical risk #2's deletion candidates).

**🟡 Medium — feature-scoped:**
9. Variant spawn/teardown: `OptimizedImage.create_for`/`#destroy` (`optimized_image.rb:35,153`), `OptimizedVideo` + `convert_video` job.
10. Email embedding (`email/sender.rb:372`, `email/styles.rb`) — broken inline images / oversized attachments.
11. Backup/restore remapping (`uploads_restorer.rb:88-127`) — restores onto a new URL scheme; must track any URL change.
12. Avatars/profiles (`user_avatar.rb`, `user_profile.rb`, `user.rb`, avatar jobs/controllers) — protected by the `after_destroy` nulling above.
13. Theme/site assets (`theme_field.rb`, `theme_svg_sprite.rb`, `site_icon_manager.rb`, `stylesheet/manager/builder.rb`, `theme_settings_manager/upload.rb`).
14. `pull_hotlinked_images` + `post_hotlinked_media` (`cooked_processor_mixin.rb:485`).

**🟢 Lower — read/serialize only, but review for URL-shape changes:**
15. Serializers in Step 4-K; site-setting/plugin surface (Step 4-J); `migrate_upload_scheme` (already URL-scheme-aware); rake tasks.

**Must re-verify after the change (matrix):** post cook→render→email; avatar upload→thumbnail→display; secure-upload toggle→ACL sync→show_secure; backup→restore→remap; S3 inventory→verification→clean_up_uploads (ensure no live deletes); video convert; theme/site-icon assets.
